title: Is It Legal to Own a Slot Machine? State-by-State Guide (2026)
excerpt: A complete breakdown of slot machine ownership laws in all 50 states, from a dealer with 33 years of experience shipping machines nationwide.
body:
We get this question more than any other. Before you start browsing machines, you need to know whether your state allows private ownership of a slot machine. The good news is that the vast majority of states do allow it.

After 33 years of shipping slot machines to homes across America, we know exactly where we can and cannot deliver. This guide reflects the laws as they stand in 2026. Keep in mind that laws change, and local jurisdictions sometimes have their own restrictions on top of state law. Always verify with your local municipality if you have any doubt.

Let us break this down into three clear categories.

![IGT S2000 Double Diamond slot machine - Used Slot Shop inventory](https://images.leadconnectorhq.com/image/f_webp/q_80/r_1200/u_https://assets.cdn.filesafe.space/hvLhtyPPvOhUoiGCUbE2/media/68753b135742b18163c5ed43.jpeg)

## Category 1: Unrestricted States (No Age Requirement)

These 13 states place no restrictions on private ownership of slot machines for personal use. You can own any machine regardless of its age or manufacture date.

- Alaska
- Arizona
- Arkansas
- Kentucky
- Maine
- Minnesota
- Nevada
- Ohio
- Rhode Island
- Texas
- Utah
- Virginia
- West Virginia

In these states, you can buy any machine we sell without concern. Whether it is a brand-new cabinet fresh off a casino floor or a classic mechanical reel machine from the 1960s, you are free to own it for personal entertainment in your home, game room, or man cave.

A few notes on specific unrestricted states. In Nevada, while personal ownership is unrestricted, operating machines for commercial gambling without a license is heavily regulated. Texas allows ownership but prohibits any form of gambling payout. Arizona — our home state — has always been friendly to private ownership, which is one reason we set up shop in Kingman back in 1992.

## Category 2: Age-Restricted States (25-Year Rule and Variations)

These 28 states allow private ownership of slot machines, but only if the machine is over a certain age. The most common threshold is 25 years old, meaning the machine must have been manufactured 25 or more years ago. Some states use different thresholds.

### 25-Year Rule States

- Alabama (25 years)
- California (25 years)
- Colorado (25 years)
- Delaware (25 years)
- Florida (25 years)
- Georgia (25 years)
- Idaho (25 years)
- Illinois (25 years)
- Iowa (25 years)
- Kansas (25 years)
- Maryland (25 years)
- Massachusetts (25 years)
- Michigan (25 years)
- Mississippi (25 years)
- Missouri (25 years)
- Montana (25 years)
- New Hampshire (25 years)
- New Mexico (25 years)
- New York (25 years)
- North Carolina (25 years)
- North Dakota (25 years)
- Oklahoma (25 years)
- Oregon (25 years)
- Pennsylvania (25 years)
- South Dakota (25 years)
- Vermont (25 years)
- Washington (25 years)
- Wyoming (25 years)

What does the 25-year rule mean in practice? In 2026, any machine manufactured in 2001 or earlier qualifies. That includes a massive range of popular machines. The IGT S2000, which debuted in 1996, qualifies in every age-restricted state. Same goes for Bally machines from the late 1990s and early Williams Gaming cabinets.

Here is where it gets interesting for buyers in these states. Many of the most sought-after home machines were built in the late 1990s and early 2000s. The IGT S2000 with classic themes like Double Diamond and Red White and Blue were manufactured through the early 2000s. Most Bally Game Maker machines from that era qualify. Williams Bluebird cabinets from 2001 and earlier are now eligible.

When you order from us, we verify the manufacture date and can provide documentation showing the machine qualifies under your state law. We will not ship a machine to an age-restricted state unless we can confirm it meets the requirement.

### States with Different Age Thresholds

A few states use a 20-year rule instead of 25. These are included in the list above at the 25-year threshold to be conservative, but it is worth noting that some states have slightly more permissive timelines. Always check your specific state statute for the exact language.

## Category 3: Prohibited States (No Private Ownership)

These 9 states prohibit private ownership of slot machines regardless of age or intended use.

- Connecticut
- Hawaii
- Indiana
- Nebraska
- South Carolina
- Tennessee
- Wisconsin
- New Jersey
- Louisiana

We cannot ship to these states. If your billing or shipping address is in one of these states, we will not process the order. No exceptions.

A few notes on the prohibited states. Indiana law is particularly strict and classifies slot machines as gambling devices regardless of whether they are used for entertainment. South Carolina updated its laws in the early 2000s after a controversy over video poker machines. Tennessee and Hawaii have historically had very restrictive gambling laws across the board. New Jersey restricts machines to licensed casinos in Atlantic City. Louisiana restricts ownership to licensed riverboat and land-based casinos.

If you live in a prohibited state but have a vacation home or property in an unrestricted state, you can have a machine shipped to that address.

## Important Distinctions

### Personal Use vs. Commercial Operation

In every state that allows private ownership, the machine must be for personal entertainment only. You cannot charge people to play, offer real payouts, or operate the machine as a commercial gambling device without proper licensing. This applies even in unrestricted states.

Most of our customers use their machines for home entertainment. They go in game rooms, home bars, man caves, and basements. Some people use them as conversation pieces in offices or businesses — that is generally fine as long as no money changes hands.

### Antique vs. Modern Machines

The 25-year rule exists because legislators viewed older machines as antiques or collectibles rather than active gambling devices. This distinction matters because it means you should keep documentation of your machine's manufacture date. We include a certificate of age with every machine we sell to an age-restricted state.

### Token vs. Coin Operation

Some states distinguish between machines that accept US currency and those that use tokens. A handful of jurisdictions are more lenient with token-operated machines. However, most modern machines we sell can be set to **free play mode**, which eliminates this concern entirely. In free play mode, you press a button to add credits without inserting anything. This is the safest configuration for home use in any state.

## How to Verify Your Local Laws

State law is just one layer. Here is how to make sure you are fully covered.

### Check State Statutes

Search for your state's gambling device or gaming machine statutes. Look for language about antique gaming devices or personal possession exemptions.

### Check County and City Ordinances

Some municipalities have their own rules. For example, certain counties in otherwise unrestricted states have local ordinances against slot machines. This is rare but worth a quick check with your city clerk or county government.

### HOA Restrictions

![IGT Game King multi-game video poker machine](https://images.leadconnectorhq.com/image/f_webp/q_80/r_1200/u_https://assets.cdn.filesafe.space/hvLhtyPPvOhUoiGCUbE2/media/687a200ece9e185839e98449.jpeg)

If you live in a homeowners association community, check your CC&Rs. Some HOAs restrict the types of items you can have visible through windows or prohibit certain categories of equipment. This usually only matters if the machine is visible from outside.

### What About Military Bases

If you live on a military installation, federal law applies rather than state law. Federal law does not explicitly prohibit personal ownership of slot machines, but individual base commanders can set policies. Check with your base JAG office.

## What Happens If You Move

We get this question regularly from military families and people who relocate for work. If you move from an unrestricted state to a prohibited state, you technically need to dispose of or store the machine outside that state. If you move to an age-restricted state, your machine needs to meet the age requirement.

The practical solution many customers use is storage. We have customers who keep machines at family members' homes in other states or in storage units in neighboring unrestricted states.

## FAQ

### Do I need a license to own a slot machine at home?

No. In states that allow private ownership, no license is required for personal use. Licensing only applies to commercial gambling operations.

### Can I put a slot machine in my business?

Generally yes, as long as it is for entertainment only and no real money gambling takes place. Bars, restaurants, and retail shops often display slot machines as decor or entertainment. However, the moment you allow patrons to gamble for money, you need a gaming license.

### What about online purchases from out of state?

The legality is determined by where the machine will physically reside, not where you buy it. We ship from Arizona, but what matters is your state's law regarding possession.

### Will the police confiscate my machine?

In legal states with proper personal use, this essentially never happens. In 33 years of selling machines, we have never had a customer in a legal state have a machine confiscated. The only cases we are aware of involved commercial gambling operations without licenses.

### My state is on the prohibited list. Can I buy one anyway?

No. We will not ship to prohibited states. We take this seriously because our reputation depends on operating legally, and we do not want our customers exposed to legal risk.

### Does the 25-year rule apply to the cabinet or the game chip?

It applies to the machine's manufacture date, which is typically the date the cabinet was built and originally configured. If a 1998 IGT S2000 had its game chip swapped in 2005, the machine is still a 1998 machine. The cabinet serial number and manufacture plate are what matter.

### Can I set the machine to pay out real money at home?

You can configure the machine however you want for personal use. Many customers enjoy playing with real quarters or tokens. The legal restriction is on operating it as a commercial gambling device — meaning you cannot charge others to play or run it as a business.

### Are video poker machines treated differently than slot machines?

In most states, video poker machines fall under the same statutes as slot machines. A few states have specific carve-outs for video poker, but we recommend treating them identically for legal purposes.

### I live in a legal state but my HOA says no. What can I do?

HOA rules are contractual, not criminal. You will not face legal trouble, but you could face fines or complaints from your HOA. Keep the machine inside and out of sight, and most HOAs will never know or care.

### How do I prove my machine's age if questioned?

We provide a certificate of manufacture date with every machine sold to age-restricted states. The machine also has a manufacturer's plate with serial number and date information. IGT, Bally, and other manufacturers maintain records that can verify production dates.
author: Used Slot Shop

title: How Much Does a Slot Machine Cost? Complete Pricing Guide (2026)
excerpt: Real pricing from a 33-year dealer: what slot machines actually cost in 2026, from budget picks under $1,000 to premium cabinets over $3,500.
body:
The short answer is anywhere from $750 to over $4,000, depending on the machine type, manufacturer, condition, and features. But the purchase price is only part of the picture. Let me give you the full breakdown based on what we actually sell every day from our showroom in Kingman, Arizona.

We have [334 machines in stock](/shop) as of this writing, spanning every price point. Here is what you can expect to spend in each category.

## Budget Machines: $750 to $1,500

![IGT Game King multi-game video poker machine](https://images.leadconnectorhq.com/image/f_webp/q_80/r_1200/u_https://assets.cdn.filesafe.space/hvLhtyPPvOhUoiGCUbE2/media/687a200ece9e185839e98449.jpeg)

This is where most first-time buyers start, and there is nothing wrong with that. A budget machine is not a bad machine. These are real casino machines that saw years of floor service and have been professionally refurbished.

### What You Get in This Range

- IGT S-Plus machines (the predecessor to the S2000) typically fall in the $750 to $1,100 range
- Older Bally reel spinners from the mid-to-late 1990s
- Basic three-reel IGT S2000 machines with common themes like Double Diamond or Red White and Blue at the lower end
- Older video poker machines like the IGT Game King in standard cabinets
- Some Bally Alpha series machines at the top of this range

### What to Expect

Machines in this range are fully functional and refurbished. They accept coins or tokens, have working bill validators in most cases, and will run for years with basic maintenance. Cosmetically, they may show more wear — minor scratches on the cabinet, some fading on the belly glass, or older-style displays. Mechanically, they are sound.

The IGT S-Plus in this range is an excellent starter machine. It is simple, reliable, and parts are still widely available. The downside is that it is an older platform with fewer game options and a more basic presentation than newer machines.

## Mid-Range Machines: $1,500 to $2,500

This is our sweet spot. About 60 percent of our sales fall in this range. You get a better cabinet, more popular game themes, and often newer technology.

### What You Get in This Range

- IGT S2000 machines with popular themes like Triple Diamond, Blazing 7s, Five Times Pay, and Wheel of Fortune
- Bally Game Maker multi-game machines
- Williams Bluebird video slots with popular titles
- Konami machines with video bonus games
- IGT Game King multi-game poker machines in slant-top or upright cabinets
- Aristocrat Mark VI machines

### What to Expect

Machines in this range look great and play great. They have been thoroughly refurbished with new or like-new components where needed. Expect clean cabinets, bright displays, responsive buttons, and reliable operation. Many machines in this range were manufactured between 1998 and 2005, putting them well within the 25-year rule for age-restricted states.

The IGT S2000 at $1,500 to $1,995 is the single most popular home slot machine in America. It combines the satisfying mechanical reel spin with a robust, proven platform that is easy to maintain.

Multi-game machines like the IGT Game King and Bally Game Maker offer tremendous value because you get dozens of games in one cabinet. A Game King with a full poker and slots suite gives you 50 or more games on one machine.

## Premium Machines: $2,500 to $4,000+

These are top-of-the-line cabinets. Either newer machines with advanced features, rare collectible machines, or high-end multi-game platforms with large screens and bonus features.

### What You Get in This Range

- IGT AVP (Trimline) machines with large LCD displays
- Bally Alpha 2 Pro V32 and similar newer platforms
- WMS Bluebird 2 cabinets with HD screens
- Aristocrat Viridian machines
- Rare or limited-edition themes
- Large multi-game platforms with 100+ game libraries
- Machines with progressive jackpot displays and bonus wheels

### What to Expect

These machines look like they just came off a casino floor. They feature LCD or LED displays, advanced sound systems, ergonomic designs, and the latest game software that manufacturers produced before the 25-year threshold. Many have bonus features like spinning wheels, second-screen bonus rounds, and progressive displays.

The Bally Alpha 2 in this range offers some of the most visually impressive gaming you can put in a home. Large high-definition screens, cinematic bonus rounds, and game themes with licensed properties.

## The Total Cost of Ownership

The machine price is the biggest expense, but it is not the only one. Here is what else you should budget for.

### Shipping: $250 to $600

Slot machines weigh between 200 and 350 pounds depending on the model. We ship via LTL freight carriers, and the cost depends on distance from our location in Kingman, Arizona.

- West Coast (California, Oregon, Washington): $250 to $350
- Mountain and Southwest states: $250 to $300
- Midwest (Texas, Illinois, Ohio): $350 to $450
- East Coast (New York, Florida, Massachusetts): $450 to $600

These are curbside delivery with liftgate service included. The freight carrier will lower the machine on its pallet to ground level at your curb. Getting it inside is your responsibility.

### Stand or Base Cabinet: $100 to $300

Slant-top machines sit on a stand or base cabinet that raises them to comfortable playing height. If you buy a slant-top, you need a stand. Upright machines are self-contained and do not require a stand.

- Basic metal stand: $100 to $150
- Matching base cabinet with storage: $200 to $300

We sell stands separately and can usually match them to your machine. Some customers build custom cabinets or install machines into bars or countertops.

### Electrical Requirements: $0 to $200

Slot machines run on standard 110V household current. You plug them into a regular wall outlet. However, there are a few considerations.

- Dedicated circuit recommended (most home circuits are shared and can handle a slot machine, but a dedicated 15-amp or 20-amp circuit is ideal)
- Surge protector strongly recommended ($30 to $50 for a quality unit)
- If you need an electrician to add an outlet: $100 to $200

Most customers plug their machine into an existing outlet with a surge protector and never have an issue.

### Tokens or Coins: $20 to $50

If you want to play with tokens rather than using **free play mode**, a bag of 200 tokens runs about $30 to $50. Alternatively, you can use real quarters. Many customers set their machines to free play mode and skip this entirely.

### Ongoing Maintenance: $0 to $100 Per Year

![Refurbished IGT S2000 ready to ship from our warehouse](https://images.leadconnectorhq.com/image/f_webp/q_80/r_1200/u_https://assets.cdn.filesafe.space/hvLhtyPPvOhUoiGCUbE2/media/6879d05b9227094875c5d253.jpeg)

Slot machines are remarkably reliable. These are commercial devices built to run 24/7 in a casino environment. In a home setting where they get played a few hours a week, they last decades.

Common maintenance items include light bulbs ($5 to $15), occasional coin mechanism cleaning (free, just needs rubbing alcohol), and very rarely a button or switch replacement ($10 to $30).

We provide [free lifetime tech support](/warranty) with every machine we sell. If something goes wrong, call us and we will walk you through the fix. Most issues can be resolved over the phone in under 15 minutes.

## Price Comparison: Dealer vs. Private Party vs. Auction

### Buying from a Dealer (Like Us)

- Higher upfront cost but machines are refurbished and tested
- Warranty included (we offer 1 year)
- Free lifetime tech support
- Documentation of machine age for legal compliance
- Professional packing and shipping
- You know exactly what you are getting

### Buying Private Party (Craigslist, Facebook Marketplace)

- Lower price, typically 30 to 50 percent less
- No warranty
- No tech support
- Unknown service history
- May have undisclosed problems (bad bill validators, failing displays, intermittent errors)
- You handle transportation
- No documentation of manufacture date

### Buying at Auction

- Lowest prices possible, sometimes $200 to $500 for machines
- No testing before purchase (usually sold as-is)
- No warranty or support
- Buyer pays all shipping from auction site
- Machines may need significant repair
- Good for experienced buyers who can do their own repairs

## What Affects Price the Most

### Game Theme

Popular themes command a premium. Wheel of Fortune, Double Diamond, and Blazing 7s are perennial favorites and sell for $200 to $500 more than obscure themes on the same platform.

### Cabinet Condition

A machine with a pristine cabinet, no scratches, clean chrome, and bright graphics will sell for more than one with visible wear. Functionality is the same, but appearance matters to most buyers.

### Platform and Age

Newer platforms with better displays and more features cost more. An IGT AVP with an LCD screen commands more than an S2000 with a CRT-style display, even if both play the same game.

### Multi-Game vs. Single Game

Multi-game machines offer better value per game but cost more upfront. A single-game S2000 might be $1,295 while a multi-game Game King is $1,995 — but the Game King has 50 or more games built in.

## Our Recommendation by Budget

### Under $1,500 Total Budget

Go with an IGT S-Plus or entry-level S2000 in the $995 to $1,200 range. Add $350 for shipping and $30 for a surge protector. You will have a real casino machine for under $1,500 all in.

### $1,500 to $2,500 Total Budget

This opens up the full S2000 lineup with popular themes, or a multi-game machine. Expect $1,500 to $1,800 for the machine, $350 to $500 for shipping, and $100 to $200 for a stand if needed.

### $2,500 to $4,000 Total Budget

You are in premium territory. Consider a Bally Alpha, WMS Bluebird, or high-end multi-game platform. These are showpiece machines that will be the centerpiece of any game room.

### $4,000+ Total Budget

At this level, consider buying two machines. A pair of complementary machines — say a reel spinner and a video bonus machine — gives you variety and makes your game room feel like a mini casino.
author: Used Slot Shop

title: The Complete Slot Machine Buyer's Guide: What to Know Before You Buy
excerpt: Everything you need to know before buying a slot machine for your home, from a dealer who has sold thousands of machines since 1992.
body:
Buying a slot machine is not like buying a television or a piece of furniture. It is a 275-pound piece of commercial equipment that was engineered to run nonstop in a casino. When you bring one home, you are getting an incredibly well-built entertainment device — but you need to go in with the right information.

We have sold thousands of machines since opening our doors in Kingman, Arizona in 1992. In that time, we have helped everyone from first-time buyers who just want something fun in the basement to serious collectors with dedicated game rooms housing a dozen machines. This guide covers everything we wish every buyer knew before making their first purchase.

## Step 1: Check Your State's Laws

![Refurbished IGT S2000 ready to ship from our warehouse](https://images.leadconnectorhq.com/image/f_webp/q_80/r_1200/u_https://assets.cdn.filesafe.space/hvLhtyPPvOhUoiGCUbE2/media/6879d05b9227094875c5d253.jpeg)

Before anything else, confirm that private ownership is legal in your state. The United States breaks down into three categories.

Thirteen states allow unrestricted ownership with no age requirement on the machine. These include Arizona, Nevada, Texas, Ohio, and Alaska among others. You can own any machine regardless of when it was made.

Twenty-eight states allow ownership of machines that are 25 or more years old. In 2026, that means any machine manufactured in 2001 or earlier. This covers the vast majority of machines we sell, including all IGT S2000 machines, most Bally Game Makers, early Williams Bluebirds, and all mechanical reel machines.

Nine states prohibit private ownership entirely. These include Indiana, Connecticut, South Carolina, Tennessee, Hawaii, Nebraska, Wisconsin, New Jersey, and Louisiana. We cannot ship to these states.

We verify legal compliance on every order. If you are in an age-restricted state, we confirm the machine's manufacture date and provide documentation.

## Step 2: Understand the Types of Machines

Slot machines fall into several broad categories. Understanding the differences will help you choose what is right for your space and your taste.

### Mechanical Reel Machines (Reel Spinners)

These are the classic slot machines with physical spinning reels. You pull the handle or press the spin button, and three or more reels physically spin and stop. The satisfying click-click-click of stopping reels is what most people picture when they think of a slot machine.

Examples include the IGT S2000 with games like Double Diamond, Red White and Blue, and Blazing 7s. Also includes older machines like the IGT S-Plus and some Bally reel machines.

Pros: Authentic feel, mechanical satisfaction, simpler operation, easier to maintain, classic look.

Cons: Limited game variety per machine (usually one game per machine), smaller payline options, no video bonus rounds.

### Video Slot Machines

These use a video screen to display virtual reels and bonus features. They offer more paylines (often 20 to 50), animated bonus rounds, second-screen features, and more complex game play.

Examples include WMS Bluebird machines with games like Wizard of Oz and Zeus, Konami video slots, and Bally Alpha video platforms.

Pros: More game variety, exciting bonus features, better graphics, often multi-game capable.

Cons: More complex electronics, potentially more expensive repairs, larger cabinets, can feel less authentic to purists.

### Video Poker Machines

These play draw poker, usually with multiple game variations available on a single machine. The IGT Game King is the gold standard here, offering 50 or more poker and slot games on one platform.

Pros: Skill element to gameplay, many game variations, excellent for poker enthusiasts, strong replay value.

Cons: Different appeal than traditional slots, smaller audience.

### Multi-Game Machines

These platforms offer multiple games selectable from a menu. You get variety without buying multiple machines. The IGT Game King, Bally Game Maker, and various multi-game platforms fall in this category.

Pros: Maximum variety, great value, one machine does the work of many.

Cons: Higher purchase price, more complex software, some purists prefer dedicated single-game machines.

### Upright vs. Slant-Top

This describes the physical cabinet style. An upright machine stands on its own with the screen at eye level — think of a tall refrigerator-shaped cabinet. A slant-top has the screen angled toward you at waist level and requires a stand or base cabinet to bring it to comfortable height.

Uprights are self-contained and take up a standard footprint. Slant-tops offer a more ergonomic playing angle and are the newer casino standard, but they need that extra stand.

## Step 3: Know the Manufacturers

Not all slot machines are created equal. Here is what you need to know about the major manufacturers.

### IGT (International Game Technology)

The 800-pound gorilla of the slot machine world. IGT makes more machines than anyone else and has for decades. Their S2000 platform is the most popular home machine ever. They also make the Game King video poker platform and the AVP/Trimline video slot series.

IGT machines are reliable, well-documented, and have the widest parts availability of any manufacturer. If you are a first-time buyer, an IGT machine is the safest choice.

### Bally Technologies

Now part of Scientific Games, Bally has been making gaming machines since the 1930s. Their Alpha series of video machines offers stunning graphics and popular game themes. The Game Maker multi-game platform is excellent for buyers who want variety.

Bally machines tend to have slightly higher-end construction and aesthetics. They cost a bit more but the build quality justifies it.

### WMS (Williams Gaming)

WMS built some of the most creative and entertaining video slots ever made. Their Bluebird and Bluebird 2 cabinets house games with elaborate bonus features and licensed themes. If you want a machine with exciting bonus rounds and creative gameplay, WMS is the way to go.

The downside is that WMS machines can be more complex to service and parts are somewhat less available than IGT.

### Aristocrat

An Australian company that has gained massive US market share. Known for games like Buffalo, 5 Dragons, and various linked progressive themes. Their Mark VI and Viridian cabinets are well-built and popular.

### Konami

Yes, the same Konami that makes video games. Their slot machines feature clean designs and popular themes. Good mid-range option with solid reliability.

### Ainsworth

Another Australian manufacturer with growing US presence. Known for quality construction and entertaining game themes. Good value in the mid-range pricing.

### Aruze

Japanese manufacturer with some unique cabinet designs. Less common in the US home market but produces quality machines.

## Step 4: Understand What Refurbished Means

Every machine we sell has been refurbished. This is not the same as used. Here is what our refurbishment process involves.

### Cleaning and Inspection

Every machine is completely disassembled. The cabinet is cleaned inside and out. All components are inspected for wear, damage, or impending failure.

### Component Replacement

Anything worn or questionable gets replaced. This commonly includes light bulbs, button switches, door gaskets, and coin mechanism parts. Bill validators are tested and rebuilt if needed.

### Electronics Testing

The main board, game board, and all electronic subsystems are powered up and tested. Error codes are cleared. Memory batteries are checked and replaced if low. Power supplies are tested under load.

### Display Calibration

CRT monitors are adjusted for brightness, focus, and color. LCD screens are checked for dead pixels or backlight issues.

### Game Testing

Every machine is played through multiple cycles to verify correct operation. Payouts are verified against the pay table. All features and bonus rounds are triggered and tested.

### Cosmetic Restoration

Cabinets are touched up where needed. Chrome is polished. Glass panels are cleaned. Decals are replaced if damaged. The goal is a machine that looks good and operates perfectly.

After refurbishment, every machine is play-tested for a minimum of several hours before being approved for sale.

## Step 5: Space and Electrical Requirements

### Physical Dimensions

A typical upright slot machine measures approximately 20 inches wide, 22 inches deep, and 42 to 52 inches tall depending on the model. With a slant-top on a stand, total height is similar.

You need at least 24 inches of width to accommodate the machine, and ideally 30 inches so you can open doors for service access. Depth should allow at least 6 inches behind the machine for air circulation and cable access.

### Weight

Most machines weigh between 200 and 350 pounds. An IGT S2000 weighs approximately 275 pounds. Video machines with larger cabinets can approach 350 pounds. Make sure your floor can handle the weight — any standard residential floor will, but if you are putting it on a second floor, keep it near a wall rather than the center of the room.

### Electrical

All machines run on standard 110V/120V household current (standard US wall outlet). They draw between 2 and 5 amps depending on the machine. A dedicated 15-amp circuit is recommended but not required. Use a quality surge protector.

Do not plug a slot machine into a power strip with other high-draw devices. Give it its own outlet or share only with low-draw items like lamps.

### Heat and Ventilation

Slot machines generate some heat, particularly older CRT-equipped models. Allow at least 6 inches of clearance behind and 3 inches on each side for airflow. Do not put them in enclosed cabinets or closets without ventilation.

### Noise

Slot machines make noise. Spinning reels click, coins drop into the tray (if coin-operated), and speakers play music and sound effects. Most machines have a volume control. In coin mode, the hopper payout is the loudest component. In free-play mode, machines are much quieter since there is no coin handling.

## Step 6: What Is Included With Your Machine

When you buy from us, here is what comes with your machine.

![IGT S2000 Double Diamond slot machine - Used Slot Shop inventory](https://images.leadconnectorhq.com/image/f_webp/q_80/r_1200/u_https://assets.cdn.filesafe.space/hvLhtyPPvOhUoiGCUbE2/media/68753b135742b18163c5ed43.jpeg)

- The complete machine with all components installed
- Power cord
- Keys (all locks keyed alike for convenience)
- Bill validator (accepts $1, $5, $10, $20 bills)
- Coin handling mechanism (hopper, acceptor)
- All original glass panels and displays
- Operating manual or instruction card where available
- Certificate of manufacture date (for age-restricted states)
- **1-year warranty** on parts and labor guidance
- Free lifetime tech support by phone

Stands for slant-top machines are sold separately. Tokens are available for purchase if you prefer them over quarters.

## Step 7: Our Warranty and Tech Support

### 1-Year Parts Warranty

Every machine we sell comes with a full 1-year warranty. If any component fails during normal use within the first year, we send you the replacement part at no charge. This covers electronics, mechanical components, displays, and power supplies. It does not cover physical damage from drops or mishandling.

### Free Lifetime Tech Support

This is what separates us from other sellers. Buy a machine from us, and you can call us for help anytime — whether it is one month or ten years after purchase. Our technicians have decades of combined experience with every platform we sell.

Most issues can be resolved over the phone in under 15 minutes. We will walk you through diagnostics, part replacement, and error code clearing. If you need a part after the warranty period, we sell them at cost.

## Step 8: Red Flags When Buying Elsewhere

If you are considering buying from someone other than an established dealer, watch for these warning signs.

### No Warranty Offered

Any seller confident in their product will offer at least some warranty. No warranty means they do not stand behind what they sell.

### Cannot Verify Manufacture Date

If a seller cannot tell you exactly when a machine was made, they either do not know what they have or are being evasive. This matters for legal compliance in age-restricted states.

### Stock Photos Instead of Actual Machine Photos

You should see photos of the specific machine you are buying. Stock photos hide condition issues.

### Unusually Low Prices

If a price seems too good to be true, the machine likely has undisclosed problems. A working, refurbished machine has a market value. Significantly below that suggests issues.

### No Physical Location

Be wary of sellers with no showroom or verifiable business address. A legitimate dealer has a physical location you can visit or verify.

### Pressure to Pay by Wire Transfer or Cryptocurrency

Legitimate businesses accept credit cards and standard payment methods that offer buyer protection.

### No Tech Support

A machine without tech support is a machine that will eventually become a 275-pound paperweight. These are complex devices and you will need help at some point.

## Step 9: How to Order From Us

Our process is straightforward.

### Browse and Select

Look through our current inventory online or call us to discuss what you are looking for. We have [334 machines in stock](/shop) and can usually match you with exactly what you want.

### Verify Legality

We confirm your state allows the machine you are selecting. For age-restricted states, we verify the machine meets the age requirement.

### Payment

We accept all major credit cards, debit cards, and bank transfers. Full payment is collected at time of order.

### Preparation and Shipping

Your machine is final-tested, cleaned, and prepared for shipping. We shrink-wrap it to a pallet and schedule pickup with our freight carrier. Typical ship time is 2 to 5 business days after order depending on our current volume.

### Delivery

LTL freight delivers to your curb with liftgate service. Transit time is 3 to 10 business days depending on distance. You will receive tracking information and a delivery appointment window.

### Setup

Unwrap, position, plug in, and play. We include setup guidance and are available by phone if you need help.

## Common Questions We Hear

### Can I change the game theme later?

On some platforms, yes. IGT S2000 machines can be converted to different themes by swapping the game chip and reel strips. This is a straightforward process. Multi-game machines already have multiple themes built in.

### Do I need to keep it stocked with coins?

No. Every machine we sell can be set to **free play mode**. Press a button to add credits without inserting anything. Many customers prefer this since it eliminates coin handling and reduces noise.

### Can I adjust the payout percentage?

On most machines, the payout percentage is determined by the game chip and cannot be changed by the owner without swapping chips. The factory-set percentages are typically between 92 and 98 percent, which provides entertaining play.

### How long do these machines last?

Decades. We regularly service machines from the 1980s and 1990s that are still running perfectly. In a home environment with moderate use, a well-maintained machine will outlast you.

### What if something breaks?

Call us. We provide [free lifetime tech support](/warranty). Most issues are minor — a bulb, a stuck coin, an error code that needs clearing. We walk you through the fix over the phone. If you need a part, we stock them and ship quickly.

### Can I have multiple machines on one circuit?

One machine per standard 15-amp circuit is recommended. Two machines on a 20-amp circuit is usually fine. Avoid putting three or more on a single circuit as the cumulative draw can trip the breaker, especially during simultaneous hopper payouts.

### Do I need internet for the machine to work?

No. Slot machines are completely self-contained. They do not need internet, network connections, or any external service. Plug into power and play.

### How loud is a slot machine?

With volume at medium and in free-play mode, comparable to a television at normal volume. In coin mode during payouts, the hopper dropping coins into the tray is noticeably louder — similar to a coffee grinder for a few seconds. Volume is adjustable on all machines.
author: Used Slot Shop

title: How We Ship a 275-Pound Slot Machine to Your Door
excerpt: A look inside our shipping process: how we prepare, pack, and freight a slot machine from Kingman, AZ to anywhere in the continental US.
body:
One of the first questions we hear from new customers is how exactly we get a 275-pound slot machine from our warehouse in Kingman, Arizona to their home in New Jersey, Florida, or wherever they happen to be. It is a fair question. These are not small packages.

We have been shipping machines nationwide since 1992 and have refined our process over thousands of deliveries. Here is exactly what happens from the moment you place your order to the moment the machine is sitting at your curb.

## The Machine: Weight and Dimensions

![IGT S2000 Double Diamond slot machine - Used Slot Shop inventory](https://images.leadconnectorhq.com/image/f_webp/q_80/r_1200/u_https://assets.cdn.filesafe.space/hvLhtyPPvOhUoiGCUbE2/media/68753b135742b18163c5ed43.jpeg)

A typical slot machine weighs between 200 and 350 pounds depending on the model. Here are some common examples.

- IGT S2000 upright: approximately 275 pounds, 20 inches wide, 22 inches deep, 44 inches tall
- IGT S2000 slant-top (without stand): approximately 150 pounds, 24 inches wide, 26 inches deep, 24 inches tall
- Bally Alpha upright: approximately 300 pounds, 22 inches wide, 24 inches deep, 52 inches tall
- WMS Bluebird upright: approximately 325 pounds, 24 inches wide, 25 inches deep, 54 inches tall
- IGT Game King slant-top (without stand): approximately 145 pounds

Upright machines are heavier because they contain the full cabinet, stand, and all components in one unit. Slant-tops are lighter individually but need a separate stand.

## How We Prepare Your Machine

After your machine passes final testing, our shipping team takes over.

### Securing Internal Components

The first step is making sure nothing moves inside the cabinet during transit. The coin hopper is locked in position. The bill validator is secured. Any loose internal wiring is zip-tied. The reel mechanism on mechanical machines is locked to prevent the reels from spinning freely and potentially damaging the reel strips.

On machines with CRT monitors, we take extra care. These glass tubes are sensitive to impact. We add internal foam bracing around the monitor assembly.

### Protecting the Exterior

All glass panels — the top glass, belly glass, and any lower panels — are protected with cardboard sheets taped in place. Door handles and protruding locks are padded. Chrome accents are wrapped.

### Shrink Wrapping

The entire machine gets wrapped in industrial shrink wrap. This is not the thin household stuff. This is thick, heavy-duty stretch film that holds everything together and provides a moisture barrier. We do multiple passes until the machine is completely encased.

### Palletizing

The wrapped machine is secured to a standard 48x40 shipping pallet using heavy-duty ratchet straps. The machine is positioned so its center of gravity is stable and it cannot tip. Corner boards are added to distribute strap pressure and protect cabinet edges.

The final package is a solid, stable unit that can be moved by forklift and will withstand the normal handling of freight transit.

## LTL Freight: How It Works

We ship via LTL (Less Than Truckload) freight carriers. This is different from UPS or FedEx parcel service. Here is what LTL means and why we use it.

LTL freight is a commercial shipping service where your palletized machine shares truck space with other freight shipments heading in the same direction. The carrier picks up from our dock, consolidates with other shipments at their terminal, routes it through their network, and delivers to your address.

We use LTL because it is the safest and most cost-effective way to move a 275-pound machine on a pallet. Parcel carriers like UPS and FedEx have weight limits (usually 150 pounds) and are not designed for this type of cargo.

Our primary carriers are established national freight companies with residential delivery capabilities. We choose carriers based on their track record of careful handling and reliable residential service.

## Transit Times by Region

Shipping from Kingman, Arizona to various regions of the country.

- Arizona, Nevada, Southern California: 2 to 3 business days
- Pacific Northwest (Oregon, Washington): 3 to 5 business days
- Mountain states (Colorado, Utah, Montana): 3 to 4 business days
- Texas and the South: 4 to 6 business days
- Midwest (Illinois, Ohio, Michigan): 5 to 7 business days
- East Coast (New York, Florida, Massachusetts): 7 to 10 business days

These are estimates. Actual transit time depends on the specific carrier route, terminal locations, and whether there are any delays in the network. Holiday periods and severe weather can add 1 to 3 days.

## Tracking Your Shipment

Once your machine ships, we email you the carrier name, PRO number (tracking number), and a link to track online. You can see when it reaches each terminal along the route.

Freight tracking is not as precise as parcel tracking. You will see terminal scans rather than every-stop updates. But you will know when it arrives at the terminal nearest your delivery address and when delivery is scheduled.

## Delivery Day: What to Expect

Here is how delivery day typically goes.

### Delivery Appointment

The carrier will call you to schedule a delivery window, usually a 4-hour block on a specific day. This is standard for residential freight delivery. You need someone present to receive the shipment.

### The Truck

A full-size freight truck (similar to a large box truck or semi trailer) will arrive at your address. The driver has your palletized machine aboard.

### Liftgate Service

All our shipments include liftgate service. The liftgate is a hydraulic platform on the back of the truck that lowers your pallet from truck bed height (about 4 feet) down to ground level. Without a liftgate, you would need a forklift or loading dock at your home — which nobody has.

The driver operates the liftgate and moves the pallet to it using a pallet jack. The platform lowers smoothly to curb level.

![IGT Game King multi-game video poker machine](https://images.leadconnectorhq.com/image/f_webp/q_80/r_1200/u_https://assets.cdn.filesafe.space/hvLhtyPPvOhUoiGCUbE2/media/687a200ece9e185839e98449.jpeg)

### Curbside Delivery

Standard freight delivery is curbside. That means the driver gets the pallet to your curb, driveway apron, or as close to your door as the truck can reach on a paved surface. The driver will not bring the machine inside your home, up stairs, or across your lawn.

This is the standard across the freight industry for residential deliveries. The driver is one person with a pallet jack — they cannot maneuver a 275-pound machine into a house.

### Inspection

Before signing the delivery receipt, inspect the exterior wrapping. If you see obvious damage to the shrink wrap or pallet — torn wrapping, crushed corners, a broken pallet — note it on the delivery receipt as damaged. Take photos. Then contact us immediately. Noting damage on the receipt is critical for any freight claim.

If the exterior looks fine, sign and accept. You can unwrap and fully inspect later.

## Getting the Machine Inside

This is the part that is on you, but we have helped thousands of customers figure it out. Here are the most common approaches.

### The Two-Person Dolly Method

This is what most customers use. Rent or buy a heavy-duty appliance dolly (hand truck) rated for at least 300 pounds. Remove the machine from the pallet and shrink wrap at the curb. Tip the machine onto the dolly and wheel it in. This takes two people — one to control the dolly and one to stabilize.

An appliance dolly with stair-climbing wheels makes steps manageable. Slot machines have a high center of gravity, so go slow and keep the machine tipped well back on the dolly.

### The Furniture Mover Method

Hire two movers for an hour. Many local moving companies offer small jobs or hourly rates. Tell them it is a 275-pound arcade-style machine on a pallet at your curb and you need it moved to a specific room. Cost is typically $75 to $150 for an hour of labor.

### The Friend and Pizza Method

Round up a strong friend or two, get a furniture dolly or appliance dolly, and take your time. This is a common approach and works fine as long as you go slowly and plan your path in advance. Measure doorways (you need at least 24 inches clear), remove doors from hinges if needed, and clear the path completely.

### For Slant-Top Machines

These are much easier since the machine head and stand are separate pieces. The machine head weighs 145 to 160 pounds and the stand is 50 to 80 pounds. Two people can carry each piece separately without special equipment.

## Tips for a Smooth Delivery

- Have your delivery path planned before the truck arrives
- Measure all doorways, hallways, and turns
- Remove any doors that are tight fits
- Clear the path of rugs, furniture, and obstacles
- Have the dolly or movers ready for the same day
- Do not leave the machine on the pallet outside overnight — weather and theft are both risks
- If you have a garage, consider having the driver place the pallet there instead of at the curb (some drivers will accommodate this if the truck can reach)

## What About White Glove Delivery?

Some customers ask about inside delivery or white glove service where professionals bring the machine to a specific room and set it up. This is available but significantly more expensive — typically $300 to $500 on top of standard shipping. It must be arranged in advance.

If you want white glove delivery, let us know at the time of order. We can either arrange it through the freight carrier (some offer this as an add-on) or coordinate with a local delivery service at your end.

## Shipping Cost Breakdown

Shipping costs depend on distance from Kingman, Arizona and current freight market rates.

- Local Southwest (AZ, NV, Southern CA): $250 to $300
- West Coast and Mountain: $275 to $375
- Midwest and South: $350 to $450
- Northeast and Southeast: $450 to $600

These include liftgate and residential delivery surcharges, which are standard freight accessorial charges. Rural delivery addresses may incur a small additional fee from the carrier.

We quote exact shipping at the time of order based on your zip code. No surprises after the fact.
author: Used Slot Shop

title: IGT S2000: Why It's America's Most Popular Home Slot Machine
excerpt: The IGT S2000 is the best-selling home slot machine in the country. Here's why collectors and casual buyers alike keep choosing this proven platform.
body:
If there is one machine that defines the home slot machine market, it is the IGT S2000. We have sold more S2000 machines than any other platform in our 33 years of business, and it is not close. There is a reason this machine dominates — actually, there are about a dozen reasons, and we are going to cover all of them.

## A Brief History of the S2000

IGT — International Game Technology — introduced the S2000 platform in 1996 as a replacement for their wildly successful S-Plus series. At the time, the S-Plus was the industry standard mechanical reel machine found in virtually every casino in America. The S2000 took everything good about the S-Plus and improved it.

![IGT Game King multi-game video poker machine](https://images.leadconnectorhq.com/image/f_webp/q_80/r_1200/u_https://assets.cdn.filesafe.space/hvLhtyPPvOhUoiGCUbE2/media/687a200ece9e185839e98449.jpeg)

The S2000 featured a more modular design, better serviceability, improved sound, brighter lighting, and a standardized game chip system that made it easy for casinos to swap between game themes. It also introduced the now-standard IGT universal mounting system for toppers, belly glass, and reel glass panels.

From 1996 through the early 2000s, IGT produced hundreds of thousands of S2000 machines. They went into casinos across the country — from the Las Vegas Strip to tribal casinos in Oklahoma to riverboats in Mississippi. When those machines eventually left casino service (the typical casino floor life for a slot machine is 5 to 8 years), they entered the secondary market.

That secondary market is where we come in. We started buying S2000 machines in the early 2000s as casinos rotated them out for newer platforms. Two decades later, they remain our best-selling machine.

## Why Collectors and Home Buyers Love the S2000

### It Feels Like a Real Casino Machine

Because it is one. This is not a toy, a replica, or a novelty item. The S2000 in your game room is the exact same machine that sat on a casino floor for years. When you pull the handle and those reels spin, you get the genuine casino experience — the weight of the handle, the acceleration of the reels, the progressive deceleration as they stop one by one, the satisfying click into position. No home gaming device replicates this feel because they are not this machine.

### Mechanical Reels

The S2000 uses actual physical reels — aluminum hubs with printed reel strips mounted on stepper motors. The reels spin at high speed and brake into position magnetically. This mechanical action creates a tactile, audiovisual experience that no video screen can match.

There is a fundamental satisfaction in watching physical objects spin and stop. It engages your senses in a way that digital displays simply do not. This is why collectors who can afford any machine on the market often choose a mechanical reel S2000 over a flashy video machine.

### Proven Reliability

The S2000 was engineered to run 24 hours a day, 7 days a week, in a casino environment where downtime costs money. Every component was designed for tens of thousands of hours of operation. In a home environment where the machine gets played a few hours a week, these machines are essentially indestructible.

We have S2000 machines in customer homes that have been running 20-plus years with nothing more than occasional light bulb changes. The stepper motors rarely fail. The main board is solid-state and robust. The power supply is overbuilt for the load it carries.

### Easy to Service

When something does eventually need attention, the S2000 is the easiest slot machine to work on. The cabinet opens with a single key. All components are accessible. Bulbs, buttons, and coin mechanisms can be swapped by anyone with basic hand tools. The game chip is a simple plug-in EPROM that slots into a clearly marked socket.

We provide [free lifetime tech support](/warranty), and most S2000 issues can be resolved over the phone in under 10 minutes. The most common calls we get are for error codes that clear by opening and closing the main door, coin jams that take 30 seconds to clear, or bulbs that need replacing.

### Parts Availability

Because IGT made so many S2000 machines, the parts ecosystem is massive. Every component is still available — reel mechanisms, stepper motors, game chips, button panels, coin acceptors, hoppers, power supplies, glass panels, toppers, and every small piece in between. If anything ever fails, we stock the replacement.

This is an important consideration that many buyers overlook. Buying an obscure machine from a small manufacturer is fine until something breaks and nobody makes the part anymore. With the S2000, that will never be a problem.

### Game Theme Variety

IGT produced the S2000 with over a hundred different game themes. You can find virtually any classic slot game on this platform.

### Legal in All Age-Restricted States

Since the S2000 was manufactured from 1996 through the early 2000s, every unit is well past the 25-year threshold required in age-restricted states. This means an S2000 is legal to own in every state that allows slot machine ownership — all 41 of them.

## Popular S2000 Game Themes

These are our top sellers and the themes customers request most often.

### Double Diamond

The single most popular slot game theme ever made. Simple three-reel gameplay with a single payline. The Double Diamond symbol doubles your win, and two of them quadruple it. This game has been in continuous casino production since the 1980s and the S2000 version is the definitive edition. Clean, classic, and endlessly replayable.

### Red White and Blue

Another IGT classic that has been a casino staple for decades. The patriotic theme features red, white, and blue 7s in various combinations. Simple payline structure with satisfying top jackpots. This is the quintessential American slot machine game.

### Triple Diamond

Similar to Double Diamond but with Triple Diamond symbols that multiply wins by 3x (or 9x with two). Slightly higher volatility than Double Diamond, which means bigger wins but less frequently. A favorite among players who like a bit more risk and reward.

### Blazing 7s

One of the most recognizable slot games in history. Features single, double, and triple Blazing 7 symbols with escalating payouts. The signature red, white, and blue color scheme and bold graphics make this an iconic looking machine. Available in single-line and multi-line versions.

### Five Times Pay

A multiplier game where the 5x symbol multiplies wins by five. Two 5x symbols multiply by 25x. The math produces exciting big-win potential on a single payline game. Popular with players who like high volatility.

### Wild Cherry

A classic fruit machine theme with cherries, bars, and 7s. This game has a nostalgic appeal that connects to the earliest days of slot machines. Clean graphics and straightforward gameplay.

### Wheel of Fortune

The licensed Wheel of Fortune theme on the S2000 includes a motorized bonus wheel on top of the machine that spins for bonus payouts. This is the most expensive S2000 variant because of the added bonus hardware, but it is also one of the most entertaining. The wheel spinning overhead creates genuine excitement.

## Coin vs. Ticket-In Ticket-Out (TITO)

S2000 machines come in two payout configurations.

### Coin Operation

The original configuration accepts coins (quarters are standard) or tokens and pays out via a coin hopper. Insert quarters, spin, and wins are paid in actual coins dropping into the tray. This is the most authentic casino experience and what most home buyers prefer.

The downside is noise — a hopper payout of 50 or 100 coins is loud. The upside is that nothing beats the sound of coins hitting the metal tray. It is pure dopamine.

### TITO (Ticket-In Ticket-Out)

![Refurbished IGT S2000 ready to ship from our warehouse](https://images.leadconnectorhq.com/image/f_webp/q_80/r_1200/u_https://assets.cdn.filesafe.space/hvLhtyPPvOhUoiGCUbE2/media/6879d05b9227094875c5d253.jpeg)

Later S2000 machines were converted to TITO, which uses printed paper tickets instead of coins. Insert a ticket or bill, play, and wins are printed as a ticket you can reinsert. This is quieter and eliminates coin handling.

For home use, TITO machines work fine but many customers miss the coin experience. A good compromise is a coin machine set to **free play mode** — you get the coin hardware for authenticity but add credits with a button press for convenience.

## What to Look For When Buying an S2000

### Cabinet Condition

Look at the overall cosmetic condition. Minor scratches and wear are normal on a machine that saw casino service. Heavy damage, rust, or missing panels suggest the machine was stored improperly.

### Reel Condition

The reel strips should be clean, bright, and free of tears or peeling. Faded reel strips work fine mechanically but look poor. Replacement strips are available if needed.

### Display Quality

Most S2000 machines have a small credit and bet display plus a VFD (vacuum fluorescent display) for messages. These should be bright and fully functional with no dead segments.

### Button Responsiveness

All buttons should respond with a clean tactile click. Mushy or unresponsive buttons indicate worn switches, which are cheap and easy to replace.

### Coin Mechanism

If the machine has a coin acceptor and hopper, both should be tested. Coin jams are the most common issue with used machines and usually indicate a mechanism that needs cleaning.

### Game Chip

Confirm what game theme is installed. The game chip determines the game. If you want a specific theme, make sure it matches.

## Price Range

S2000 machines in our inventory range from $995 to $1,995 depending on game theme, condition, and configuration.

- Basic themes in good condition: $995 to $1,295
- Popular themes (Double Diamond, Red White and Blue) in excellent condition: $1,295 to $1,595
- Premium themes (Wheel of Fortune, multi-denomination) in excellent condition: $1,595 to $1,995

At any price point in this range, you are getting a fully refurbished, tested, and warranted machine with free lifetime tech support.

## Maintenance Tips for S2000 Owners

The S2000 needs very little maintenance in a home environment, but a few simple practices will keep it running perfectly for decades.

### Monthly

- Wipe down the cabinet exterior with a damp cloth
- Clean the glass panels with standard glass cleaner
- Check that all lights are functioning

### Every 6 Months

- Open the main door and blow out any dust with compressed air
- Check the coin mechanism for debris if you use coins
- Inspect the reel strips for any signs of peeling (rare but worth checking)

### Annually

- Clean the coin acceptor mechanism with isopropyl alcohol and a soft cloth
- Check the hopper for coin dust buildup
- Verify the backup battery voltage on the main board (CR2032 battery, easy to replace)
- Inspect all cable connections for corrosion or looseness

### Things That Will Shorten Your Machine's Life

- Cigarette smoke (gums up mechanisms and yellows plastics)
- Excessive humidity (causes corrosion on circuit boards)
- Power surges (always use a surge protector)
- Leaving it powered on 24/7 (unnecessary wear; turn it off when not in use)

## The Bottom Line

The IGT S2000 earned its reputation as America's most popular home slot machine through a combination of authentic casino feel, proven reliability, easy maintenance, abundant parts availability, and classic game themes that never get old. Whether you are buying your first machine or your fifth, the S2000 belongs in the conversation.

We keep 50 to 75 S2000 machines in stock at all times across various themes and configurations. Call us and tell us what game you want — chances are good we have it ready to ship.
author: Used Slot Shop

title: Refurbished vs. Used Slot Machines: What's the Difference?
excerpt: Learn the critical differences between refurbished and used slot machines, what the refurbishment process involves, and why it matters for home buyers.
body:
If you have been shopping for a slot machine for your home game room, you have probably noticed that some sellers list machines as "used" while others say "refurbished." These are not interchangeable terms. The difference between a used slot machine and a refurbished one can mean the difference between a machine that works perfectly for years and one that becomes an expensive paperweight within months.

After 33 years in this business, we have seen every shortcut and every honest approach to selling pre-owned slot machines. Let us break down exactly what these terms mean, what you should expect from each, and why the price difference exists for good reason.

## What "Used" Actually Means

![Refurbished IGT S2000 ready to ship from our warehouse](https://images.leadconnectorhq.com/image/f_webp/q_80/r_1200/u_https://assets.cdn.filesafe.space/hvLhtyPPvOhUoiGCUbE2/media/6879d05b9227094875c5d253.jpeg)

A used slot machine is exactly what it sounds like. It is a machine that has been removed from a casino floor and is being sold in whatever condition it arrived in. The seller may have powered it on to verify it boots up, but that is typically the extent of any testing.

Used machines come with all the wear and tear of their casino life. That could mean ten years of cigarette smoke residue coating the interior components. It could mean a bill acceptor that jams on every third bill. It could mean a monitor that is starting to dim or show burn-in from displaying the same attract screen for 80,000 hours.

The key thing to understand about used machines is that they are sold in "as-is" condition. Whatever problems exist when you receive it are your problems. Some used machines work perfectly. Others need hundreds of dollars in repairs before they will function reliably. You are rolling the dice, which is ironic given what you are buying.

Used machines typically come from a few sources. Casino liquidations are the most common, where an entire floor of machines gets pulled and sold in bulk to dealers or directly to the public. Estate sales are another source. Occasionally you will find machines from route operators who ran them in bars or convenience stores.

The condition varies wildly. A machine pulled from a high-end Las Vegas casino that refreshes its floor every three years will be in dramatically better shape than one that spent twelve years in a smoky truck stop in rural Nevada.

## What "Refurbished" Means

A refurbished slot machine has been through a comprehensive restoration process designed to bring it as close to factory-new condition as possible. This is not a quick wipe-down and a prayer. A proper refurbishment involves 18 to 40 hours of skilled technician labor depending on the machine type and its starting condition.

At our shop, refurbishment is a systematic process with defined standards. Every machine goes through the same checklist regardless of how good or bad it looks when it arrives. We have been refining this process since 1992 and it covers every component that can wear, fail, or degrade.

Refurbished means the machine has been disassembled, inspected, repaired or replaced where needed, reassembled, tested, and certified ready for years of reliable home use. It is the difference between buying a car from a corner lot with an "as-is" sign and buying one from a dealership that did a 150-point inspection.

## Our Refurbishment Process Step by Step

When a machine arrives at our Kingman facility, it goes through a documented multi-stage process.

### Stage 1: Initial Assessment and Disassembly

We power on the machine and document every error code, every cosmetic issue, and every functional problem. Then we disassemble it down to individual components. The main board comes out. The power supply comes out. The bill acceptor, coin mechanism, hopper, reel assembly or video card, all of it gets pulled and laid out on the bench.

### Stage 2: Cleaning

Every component gets cleaned individually. Circuit boards get inspected under magnification and cleaned with appropriate electronics cleaner. The cabinet interior gets stripped of decades of dust and residue. The exterior gets detailed. The glass or acrylic panels get polished or replaced if scratched beyond acceptable standards.

You would be amazed at what accumulates inside a casino slot machine. We have pulled out casino chips, cigarette butts, entire colonies of dead insects, and once a small lizard that had somehow gotten inside a machine shipped from Southern Nevada.

### Stage 3: Component Testing and Replacement

This is where the real work happens. Every replaceable component gets tested against factory specifications.

The power supply gets load-tested. If voltage regulation is out of spec or if capacitors show any signs of bulging or leaking, the entire power supply gets replaced. We do not recap old power supplies. A new one costs us $45 to $80 depending on the machine type. It is not worth the risk of a marginal unit failing six months later.

The bill acceptor gets completely rebuilt. New belts, new sensors cleaned and calibrated, new stacker motor if needed. We test with every denomination of bill in multiple conditions including slightly wrinkled bills because that is what real use looks like.

For reel machines, every reel gets inspected. The reel strips get checked for fading or peeling. The stepper motors get tested for accurate positioning. The opto sensors that read reel position get cleaned and verified.

For video machines, the monitor is the critical component. CRT monitors get checked for convergence, brightness, and burn-in. If a CRT is showing its age, we replace it with a new LCD conversion kit. LCD monitors get tested for dead pixels, backlight uniformity, and proper color reproduction.

The main logic board gets tested with diagnostic software. Memory batteries get replaced regardless of remaining charge. On IGT machines, that means a new Dallas DS1287 real-time clock chip. These batteries last about 7 to 10 years, and if they die, you lose all your machine settings and potentially the game software itself.

Door switches, belly glass, top glass, button panels, and all the small components that make up the player interface get checked and replaced if worn.

### Stage 4: Reassembly and Configuration

Once every component passes inspection, the machine gets reassembled with proper cable routing and secure connections. We configure it for home use, which means setting it to **free play mode**, adjusting the theoretical payback percentage to a home-friendly setting (usually 95 to 98 percent), and ensuring all the settings are stored properly.

### Stage 5: Burn-In Testing

The assembled machine runs for a minimum of 48 hours continuously. We monitor for intermittent errors, overheating, or any issues that only appear after extended operation. Random play testing occurs throughout this period. If any issue appears during burn-in, we go back to stage 3 for that component.

### Stage 6: Final Inspection and Documentation

![IGT S2000 Double Diamond slot machine - Used Slot Shop inventory](https://images.leadconnectorhq.com/image/f_webp/q_80/r_1200/u_https://assets.cdn.filesafe.space/hvLhtyPPvOhUoiGCUbE2/media/68753b135742b18163c5ed43.jpeg)

A second technician inspects the finished machine. Fresh eyes catch things the primary tech might overlook. The machine gets photographed and any remaining cosmetic wear gets documented so the buyer knows exactly what they are getting.

## Why Refurbished Costs More

A refurbished machine from our shop typically costs $300 to $800 more than the same model sold as-is from a liquidator. That price difference covers 18 to 40 hours of technician labor, replacement parts (typically $75 to $200 in parts per machine), new batteries, new bill acceptor components, and the testing time.

When you consider that a qualified slot technician commands $25 to $45 per hour, the math makes sense quickly. We are able to keep refurbished prices reasonable because we do this at volume with [334 machines in stock](/shop) at any given time and because our technicians have been doing this work for years. Efficiency comes with experience.

## Warranty Differences

This is where the rubber meets the road for most buyers.

Used machines sold as-is typically come with no warranty whatsoever. If the bill acceptor dies a week after delivery, that is your problem. If the monitor fails, you are sourcing parts and finding a local tech or shipping it back on your own dime.

Our refurbished machines come with a full **1-year warranty** covering all mechanical and electronic components. If something fails during normal home use within that year, we cover the repair or replacement including parts and our tech support time. We also provide free lifetime technical support, meaning you can call us in year five with a question and we will walk you through it.

That warranty is not just a piece of paper. It represents our confidence in the refurbishment process. We would not offer a year of coverage if our machines were failing at any significant rate. Our warranty claim rate runs under 4 percent annually, and most of those are minor issues resolved with a phone call and a replacement part shipped at no charge.

## When Used Might Make Sense

We are honest about this. There are situations where buying a used as-is machine can be reasonable. If you are mechanically inclined and enjoy tinkering, an as-is machine gives you a project. If you are buying a very common model where parts are cheap and plentiful, the risk is lower. If your budget is extremely tight and you understand you might need to invest additional money in repairs, used can get you in the door.

But for the vast majority of home buyers who want to plug in a machine and enjoy it without becoming a slot machine technician themselves, refurbished is the clear choice. The upfront savings of buying used often evaporate with the first repair bill.

## What to Ask Any Seller

Whether you buy from us or elsewhere, here are the questions that separate real refurbishment from marketing language.

Ask what specific work was performed. A legitimate refurbisher can give you a detailed answer. If they say "it was tested and cleaned" that is not refurbishment. That is ten minutes of work.

Ask about the power supply. Was it load-tested or replaced? Ask about the memory battery. Was it replaced? Ask about the bill acceptor. Was it rebuilt or just tested with one bill?

Ask about the warranty terms in writing. What is covered? What is excluded? Who pays shipping if warranty service is needed? How long do you have to report an issue?

Ask how long they have been in business. Refurbishing slot machines well requires deep knowledge of dozens of different platforms across multiple manufacturers spanning 30 years of technology. A newcomer simply does not have the institutional knowledge to do this work at a high level.

## The Bottom Line

Used means you are buying someone else's retired machine with all its unknown history and hidden problems. Refurbished means a skilled technician has gone through that machine systematically, fixed what needed fixing, replaced what needed replacing, and verified that everything works correctly.

The price difference is real but modest relative to the total purchase. And when you factor in the warranty, the technical support, and the peace of mind, refurbished is not just the better value. For most home buyers, it is the only option that makes sense.

We have been doing this since 1992. We refurbish every machine we sell to the same standard. Call us and we will walk you through exactly what was done to any specific machine you are considering.
author: Used Slot Shop

title: How to Set Up Your Slot Machine at Home: Complete Installation Guide
excerpt: Step-by-step guide to receiving, placing, and powering on your home slot machine, from delivery day through your first spin.
body:
You just bought a slot machine and it is on its way to your house. Exciting. But these are not exactly plug-and-play devices like a television. A slot machine is a precision electromechanical device that weighs as much as a large refrigerator and has specific requirements for safe operation.

After shipping thousands of machines to homes across the country over the past 33 years, we have developed this guide to cover everything you need to know about getting your machine from the delivery truck to its first spin. Follow these steps and your machine will reward you with years of trouble-free entertainment.

## Before Delivery Day

![IGT S2000 Double Diamond slot machine - Used Slot Shop inventory](https://images.leadconnectorhq.com/image/f_webp/q_80/r_1200/u_https://assets.cdn.filesafe.space/hvLhtyPPvOhUoiGCUbE2/media/68753b135742b18163c5ed43.jpeg)

Preparation starts before the truck arrives. You need to have your space ready, your electrical sorted out, and a plan for getting 225 to 325 pounds of machine from your front door to its final resting place.

### Choose Your Location

Slot machines need more space than most people expect. A typical upright (standing) slot machine measures approximately 30 inches wide, 24 inches deep, and 72 to 76 inches tall including the top box. A slant-top (sit-down) machine is shorter at about 48 to 52 inches tall but needs the same floor space plus room for a chair.

Beyond the machine footprint itself, you need clearance. Plan for at least 6 inches of space behind the machine for ventilation and cable access. You want at least 36 inches of clear space in front for comfortable play. The rear door of most machines swings open for maintenance, so ensure you can access the back without moving the machine.

Avoid locations with these characteristics. Direct sunlight will fade the graphics and overheat the electronics. Damp basements without climate control will corrode connectors and circuit boards. Unheated garages with temperature extremes will stress the monitor and electronic components. Anywhere near a hot tub or pool where humidity is high.

Ideal locations include climate-controlled game rooms, finished basements, home bars, man caves, or any interior room that stays between 60 and 80 degrees Fahrenheit with reasonable humidity.

### Check Your Floor

A fully loaded slot machine with a full coin hopper can weigh 350 pounds concentrated on about 5 square feet of floor space. Standard residential flooring can handle this without issue in most cases. However, if you are placing it on a raised floor, a second story, or an older structure, verify that the floor joists can handle the concentrated load.

Hardwood floors will benefit from furniture pads under the leveling feet to prevent dents. Carpet works fine but makes leveling slightly more challenging.

### Plan Your Path

Measure every doorway, hallway, and stairway between your front door and the machine's destination. The machine is roughly 30 inches wide and 24 inches deep. Standard interior doors are 30 to 32 inches wide, which means it will fit through most doorways with minimal clearance.

If you need to navigate stairs, stop and think carefully. A 300-pound machine on a stairway is genuinely dangerous. Consider whether you can remove the top box to reduce height and weight (most detach with 4 to 6 bolts). If the stairs are narrow or winding, hire professional movers experienced with heavy equipment. This is not the time for heroics with your buddy and a hand truck.

Speaking of hand trucks, a heavy-duty appliance dolly rated for at least 400 pounds is the right tool for moving a slot machine on flat ground. Regular lightweight dollies are not sufficient.

## Receiving Your Delivery

Most slot machines ship via freight carrier on a pallet, strapped and wrapped. When the delivery truck arrives, here is what to do.

### Inspect Before Signing

Before you sign the delivery receipt, inspect the exterior packaging for obvious damage. Look for holes punched through the cardboard, crushed corners, signs the pallet was dropped, or water staining. If you see damage, note it clearly on the delivery receipt before signing. Write "received with visible damage" and describe what you see. Take photos with your phone.

This documentation is critical if you need to file a freight claim. Once you sign a clean receipt, proving transit damage becomes much harder.

### Unpacking

Remove the outer packaging carefully. The machine will likely be wrapped in moving blankets or foam and secured with straps to the pallet. Remove straps and wrapping, then do a visual inspection of the machine itself before moving it off the pallet.

Check the glass panels for cracks. Check the monitor screen for damage. Verify the top box is still securely attached. Look at the coin tray and button panel for any obvious issues.

Save all packaging materials for at least 30 days in case you need to ship the machine back for any warranty reason.

## Temperature Acclimation

This step is critical and most people skip it. Do not skip it.

If your machine was shipped during cold weather or was sitting in an unheated delivery truck, the internal components may be well below room temperature. Powering on cold electronics can cause condensation to form on circuit boards as the components warm up in a room-temperature environment. Condensation on powered electronics causes short circuits and can permanently damage components.

The rule is simple. Let the machine sit unpowered in its final location for at least 4 to 6 hours before turning it on. If it was shipped in extreme cold (below freezing), give it a full 24 hours. This allows all internal components to reach room temperature gradually and any condensation to evaporate before you apply power.

During summer shipping this is less critical, but we still recommend at least 2 hours of acclimation as a standard practice.

## Positioning and Leveling

Once the machine is in its final location, it needs to be level. This matters more than you might think. Coin mechanisms rely on gravity to feed coins correctly. Reel mechanisms need to be plumb to spin freely. Bill acceptors need proper alignment to feed bills smoothly.

Most slot machines have four adjustable leveling feet on the bottom. Use a bubble level placed on top of the machine and adjust the feet until you get level in both directions (side to side and front to back).
