### Scenario B: Buy a Pre-Loaded Multi-Game Machine

- IGT Game King with 100+ games from our inventory: $1,795 to $2,295
- Shipping: $300 to $500
- Total cost: $2,095 to $2,795
- Result: A complete, tested, warrantied multi-game machine

### Scenario C: Buy Multiple Single-Game Machines

- To get 50 different game experiences, you would need 50 machines
- Even at $1,000 each, that is $50,000 plus the warehouse to store them
- Obviously not practical for home use

The conversion kit path makes the most financial sense if you already own a compatible machine and are comfortable doing the installation. If you are starting from scratch, buying a pre-loaded machine gives you the warranty and support without the DIY risk.

## Legal Considerations

Here is something many sellers will not tell you about conversion kits: there is a legal gray area.

In a regulated casino environment, every game chip must be approved by the state gaming commission. The software goes through rigorous testing and certification. Conversion kits for home use do not go through this process because home machines are not regulated the same way.

For personal home entertainment, this is not an issue. You are not operating a commercial gambling device. The games are for your enjoyment in your own home. No gaming commission is going to audit your basement.

However, if you ever plan to use the machine in a commercial setting — even a charity event or a bar — the uncertified game software could create legal exposure. Stick to certified, factory-original configurations for any commercial use.

The [state-by-state legality](/blog/is-it-legal-to-own-a-slot-machine-state-by-state-guide) of owning the machine itself is a separate question from what software runs on it. As long as the machine is legal to own in your state, the game software you run on it for personal use is your business.

## Our Recommendation

After seeing thousands of customers go through this decision, here is our advice.

If you already own an IGT Game King or Bally Game Maker and want more variety, a conversion kit is an excellent investment. The installation is simple on these platforms, the cost is reasonable, and the result is dramatic — going from 31 games to 100+ transforms the machine.

If you own a mechanical reel machine like an [IGT S2000](/blog/igt-s2000-americas-most-popular-home-slot-machine) and want multi-game capability, do not try to convert it. Instead, keep your S2000 for that classic reel-spinning experience and add a dedicated multi-game machine as a second unit. Check our [pricing guide](/blog/how-much-does-a-slot-machine-cost-pricing-guide) for what to expect to spend.

If you do not own a machine yet and know you want multi-game capability, buy a pre-loaded multi-game machine from the start. Browse our [current inventory](/shop) — we always have multiple multi-game configurations in stock with our [1-year warranty](/warranty) and free lifetime tech support.

## Where to Buy Conversion Kits

We sell conversion kits for IGT Game King platforms directly. These are tested, verified kits with installation instructions. We also provide phone support during your installation if you get stuck — that is part of our [free lifetime tech support](/warranty) commitment.

For other platforms, kits are available from various online sellers. Be cautious about quality — cheap kits from overseas sellers sometimes have compatibility issues or missing components. Buy from a seller who provides support and stands behind the product.

Have questions about whether your specific machine is a good conversion candidate? [Contact us](/contact) — we are happy to advise based on your exact model and configuration. And if you decide conversion is not the right path, we have [334 multi-game machines](/shop) in stock ready to [ship nationwide](/shipping).
author: Used Slot Shop

title: What Happens When Casinos Retire Slot Machines? The Journey From Casino Floor to Your Home
excerpt: A 33-year veteran dealer explains exactly what happens when casinos remove working slot machines — and how those machines end up refurbished and ready for your game room.
body:
Every slot machine you see in our [inventory](/shop) spent years on a real casino floor. These are not reproductions, knock-offs, or home-use imitations. They are the genuine article — the same machines that entertained thousands of casino visitors before being retired and finding a second life in someone's home.

But why would a casino get rid of a perfectly working slot machine? And what happens between the day it leaves the casino floor and the day it arrives at your door? After 33 years of buying retired machines from casinos, refurbishing them, and selling them to home buyers, we have a front-row seat to this process. Let us walk you through the entire journey.

## Why Casinos Replace Working Machines

This is the question that surprises most people. The machines are not broken. They are not worn out. In most cases, they are running perfectly. So why does a casino spend millions replacing them?

### Revenue Per Square Foot

A casino floor is real estate, and every square foot needs to earn its keep. Casino operators track revenue per machine with forensic precision. They know exactly how much each machine earns per day, per week, per month. When a machine's daily revenue drops below a threshold — even if it is still profitable — it becomes a candidate for replacement.

A newer machine with a trendy theme, better graphics, and more engaging bonus features will almost always out-earn an older machine in the same spot. The casino is not looking at whether the old machine makes money. They are looking at whether a new machine would make more money in that same square footage. The answer is almost always yes.

### Player Demand for New Games

Casino visitors want novelty. They have seen Double Diamond a thousand times. When a manufacturer releases a new game with a popular licensed theme — a hit TV show, a movie franchise, a celebrity brand — players flock to it. Casinos must constantly rotate in new titles to keep their floor feeling fresh.

This is the same psychology that drives the video game industry. Players always want the next thing. Casinos that do not refresh their floors lose visitors to competitors who do.

### Manufacturer Lease Agreements

![IGT S2000 Double Diamond - the most popular home slot machine](https://images.leadconnectorhq.com/image/f_webp/q_80/r_1200/u_https://assets.cdn.filesafe.space/hvLhtyPPvOhUoiGCUbE2/media/68753b135742b18163c5ed43.jpeg)

Many newer machines are not purchased outright by casinos — they are leased from manufacturers under revenue-sharing agreements. When the lease expires or a newer model becomes available, the leased machines go back to the manufacturer. This creates a steady stream of returned machines that eventually enter the secondary market.

### Regulatory Requirements

Some gaming jurisdictions require periodic technology updates. A state gaming commission might mandate that all machines on the floor support a certain type of player tracking system, a specific communication protocol, or a new security standard. Older machines that cannot be upgraded to meet the new requirement must be removed.

### Floor Renovations

Casinos periodically renovate sections of their floor. A redesign might change the layout, reduce total machine count in favor of table games, or shift to a different cabinet style for aesthetic consistency. Machines that do not fit the new vision get pulled regardless of performance.

## Typical Casino Floor Life: 3 to 7 Years

The average slot machine spends 3 to 7 years on a casino floor before retirement. High-performing machines in prime locations might last 7 to 10 years. Underperformers in competitive markets might be pulled after just 2 to 3 years.

During that time, the machine runs nearly 24 hours a day, 7 days a week. A machine with 5 years of casino service has accumulated roughly 40,000 hours of continuous operation. That sounds like a lot — and it is — but these machines are built for exactly that kind of duty cycle. Commercial slot machines are engineered to the same reliability standards as industrial equipment.

To put it in perspective: if you play your home machine 2 hours per day, every single day, it would take you 55 years to accumulate the same operating hours as 5 years of casino service. The machine in your game room is essentially barely broken in by commercial standards.

## What Happens at Retirement

When a casino decides to retire a group of machines, the process follows a fairly standard pattern across the industry.

### Step 1: Decommissioning

The machine is powered down and disconnected from the casino's central monitoring system. The progressive link (if any) is severed. Player tracking hardware specific to that casino is removed. The machine's memory is cleared of revenue data and player information.

### Step 2: Regulatory Compliance

In jurisdictions that track gaming devices, the casino files paperwork with the gaming commission indicating the machine is being removed from active service. The machine's gaming license for that specific location is surrendered.

### Step 3: Bulk Sale to Dealers

Casinos do not sell machines one at a time to individual buyers. They sell in bulk — often hundreds of machines at once — to licensed dealers and distributors. This is where we come in.

We have relationships with casinos and casino equipment liquidators across the country. When a property retires a bank of machines, we get the call. We evaluate what is available, negotiate pricing, and arrange transportation from the casino to our facility in Kingman, Arizona.

A typical purchase might be 50 to 200 machines from a single property. We select based on platform, condition, and game popularity. Not every retired machine is worth refurbishing. We pass on machines with obsolete platforms, rare cabinets with no parts availability, or models with limited home-market appeal.

### Step 4: Transportation

The machines are palletized at the casino and shipped via freight truck to our facility. They arrive shrink-wrapped and strapped to pallets, usually in good physical condition. Casino machines are handled carefully during removal because the casino wants to maximize their resale value.

## The Refurbishment Process

This is where a retired casino machine becomes a home-ready entertainment device. Our refurbishment process is thorough and systematic. Every machine goes through the same steps regardless of its apparent condition.

### Complete Disassembly and Inspection

We open every door, remove every accessible component, and inspect the machine inside and out. We are looking for worn parts, loose connections, corroded contacts, damaged wiring, cracked plastics, and anything that might cause a problem down the road.

Casino machines accumulate cigarette smoke residue, dust, spilled drinks, and general grime over years of public use. The first step is getting everything clean so we can see what we are actually working with.

### Electronics Testing and Repair

The main board, game board, power supply, and all electronic subsystems are tested individually. We check voltages, verify chip integrity, test memory batteries, and clear any error codes. Boards with issues are repaired at the component level or replaced entirely.

Power supplies are load-tested. A power supply might appear fine at idle but fail under the full load of a spinning reel motor and illuminated displays. We catch those failures during testing so you never encounter them at home.

### Mechanical Component Service

Reel motors are inspected and lubricated. Coin mechanisms are disassembled, cleaned, and rebuilt with new springs and contacts where needed. Bill validators are cleaned, calibrated, and tested with multiple bill denominations. Button switches are tested and replaced if they feel mushy or inconsistent.

### Display Calibration and Repair

CRT monitors on older machines are adjusted for brightness, focus, color balance, and geometry. Caps are replaced if the monitor shows signs of age (dim image, color shift, or geometric distortion). LCD displays are checked for dead pixels, backlight uniformity, and touch calibration.

![Bally slot machine in our refurbishment facility](https://images.leadconnectorhq.com/image/f_webp/q_80/r_1200/u_https://assets.cdn.filesafe.space/hvLhtyPPvOhUoiGCUbE2/media/6879c0e257dd60bea48569a9.jpeg)

### Cosmetic Restoration

The cabinet is cleaned, polished, and touched up. Chrome trim is polished or replaced. Glass panels are cleaned and inspected for chips or cracks. Belly glass and top glass are some of the most visible elements — if they are faded, scratched, or damaged, we replace them. Decals and overlays are replaced if worn.

The goal is not making the machine look brand new — that would be dishonest. The goal is making it look great. Like a well-maintained machine that has been cared for, not like one that just survived five years of 24/7 public abuse.

### Configuration for Home Use

Casino machines have configurations specific to casino operation — denomination settings, payout percentages for regulatory compliance, player tracking system integration, and more. We reconfigure each machine for optimal home use.

This includes setting the machine to accept quarters or tokens (or free-play mode), configuring the volume to reasonable levels, clearing all casino-specific data, and ensuring the bill validator works with current US currency.

### Play Testing

Every refurbished machine is played through extended testing cycles before approval. We play every game, trigger every bonus feature, test every payout level, and verify every display element. If anything is not right, it goes back to the bench.

## Why Retired Does Not Mean Broken

This is the most important point in this entire article. Casinos do not retire machines because they stop working. They retire them because newer machines earn more money per square foot.

The machine that sits in your game room was generating revenue for the casino on its last day of service. It was working perfectly. Players were sitting in front of it, feeding it bills, and enjoying the gameplay. The casino replaced it with something newer — not something that worked better.

Consider this analogy: a rental car company retires vehicles at 30,000 to 50,000 miles and buys new ones. Not because the old cars are broken, but because new cars attract more customers. The retired rental car still has 150,000 or more miles of life left in it. Same principle applies to slot machines.

When you buy a [refurbished machine](/blog/refurbished-vs-used-slot-machines-whats-the-difference) from us, you are getting a device that was engineered for 100,000+ hours of continuous operation, used for maybe 30,000 to 50,000 hours, professionally serviced, and then sent to your home where it will see perhaps 500 hours per year. The math works overwhelmingly in your favor.

## How Machines End Up at Used Slot Shop

Our specific supply chain works like this.

We maintain relationships with casinos and liquidators across Nevada, Arizona, California, and other gaming states. When properties refresh their floors, we are among the first calls they make. We have been in this business since 1992, and many of these relationships go back decades.

We are selective about what we buy. Out of a batch of 200 retired machines, we might take 80 to 120 depending on what we need for inventory. We focus on platforms with strong home-market demand: IGT S2000, Game King, Bally Alpha, WMS Bluebird, Aristocrat, Konami, and similar proven favorites.

Every machine we buy goes through our complete refurbishment process, gets our [1-year warranty](/warranty), and comes with free lifetime tech support. We currently have [334 machines in stock](/shop) representing the best of what we have sourced and refurbished.

## The Economics: Why This Is a Good Deal for Buyers

A new slot machine costs a casino $15,000 to $25,000 or more. The machine depreciates rapidly on the casino's books — most are fully depreciated within 3 to 5 years for accounting purposes, regardless of their actual condition.

When that machine is retired and sold in bulk to a dealer, the casino might receive $200 to $800 per unit depending on model and condition. The dealer (us) invests $200 to $500 in refurbishment labor and parts per machine. The machine then sells to a home buyer for $995 to $3,500 depending on model and configuration.

So a machine that cost $18,000 new and has decades of life remaining sells for $1,500 to $2,500 as a [refurbished home machine](/blog/how-much-does-a-slot-machine-cost-pricing-guide). That is outstanding value. You are getting commercial-grade entertainment equipment built to standards far exceeding any consumer product, at a fraction of its original cost.

## What to Look For When Buying a Retired Casino Machine

If you are buying from a reputable dealer (like us), the machine has been professionally refurbished and you do not need to worry about most of these factors. But for your own education — or if you are considering buying from a private seller — here is what matters.

### Platform Age and Parts Availability

Older platforms may be harder to service long-term as parts become scarce. The IGT S2000 has excellent parts availability because millions were produced. More exotic platforms may not. Ask about parts availability before buying.

### Casino of Origin

Machines from well-maintained, high-end properties tend to be in better condition than those from smaller, lower-budget operations. Not a hard rule, but a general trend.

### Service History

A machine that was regularly serviced during its casino life will be in better internal condition than one that was run until it failed. Dealers who buy from quality properties get better raw material to refurbish.

### Smoke Exposure

Casinos in smoking-allowed jurisdictions produce machines with significantly more internal contamination. Smoke residue coats components and can accelerate corrosion. Proper refurbishment removes this, but it is one reason why thorough cleaning is so important.

## Ready to Own a Piece of Casino History?

Every machine in our showroom has a story. It spent years entertaining visitors on a real casino floor. It paid out jackpots, triggered bonus rounds, and created memories for thousands of players. Now it is ready to do the same in your home.

Browse our [current inventory of 334 machines](/shop), read our [complete buyer's guide](/blog/complete-slot-machine-buyers-guide), or review our [warranty and support](/warranty) to understand exactly what you get when you buy from us. We [ship nationwide](/shipping) and are happy to answer any questions — just [contact us](/contact).

Your next slot machine had a previous life. That is not a drawback. That is proof it was built to last.
author: Used Slot Shop

title: Slot Machine Dimensions and Weight Guide: Will It Fit In Your Space?
excerpt: Exact measurements and weights for every major slot machine cabinet type, plus doorway clearance, floor load, and ceiling height requirements from a 33-year dealer.
body:
We get this question every single day: will it fit? Will it fit through my door, will it fit in my game room, will my floor hold it? These are critical questions to answer before you buy — not after a 300-pound machine is sitting on your curb with no way to get inside.

After 33 years of selling slot machines and helping customers plan their installations, we know the exact dimensions of every major cabinet type. Here is the definitive reference guide.

## IGT S2000 Upright

The [IGT S2000](/blog/igt-s2000-americas-most-popular-home-slot-machine) is the most common machine we sell, and its dimensions make it one of the easiest to accommodate in a home setting.

![IGT S2000 Double Diamond upright cabinet](https://images.leadconnectorhq.com/image/f_webp/q_80/r_1200/u_https://assets.cdn.filesafe.space/hvLhtyPPvOhUoiGCUbE2/media/68753b135742b18163c5ed43.jpeg)

- **Width:** 20.5 inches
- **Depth:** 22 inches
- **Height:** 46 inches
- **Weight:** 250 to 275 pounds

The S2000 upright is remarkably compact for a full-size casino machine. At just over 20 inches wide, it fits through any standard doorway with room to spare. The 46-inch height puts the reel window at comfortable eye level for a seated player, and works well standing too.

This machine fits in spots where you might put a small bookcase or end table. It does not dominate a room. Many customers place two or three S2000 uprights side by side — a row of three takes up just over 5 feet of wall space.

## IGT S2000 Slant-Top

The slant-top version of the S2000 has the screen angled toward the player at roughly 30 degrees. It requires a stand or base cabinet.

- **Machine only width:** 24 inches
- **Machine only depth:** 24 inches
- **Machine only height:** 18 inches (the cabinet alone, without stand)
- **On standard stand — total height:** 42 to 48 inches (depending on stand)
- **On standard stand — total depth:** 26 inches
- **Weight (machine only):** 150 to 175 pounds
- **Weight (with stand):** 200 to 225 pounds

The slant-top is wider than the upright because the angled display requires a wider chassis. It also projects further from the wall due to the angled screen. Plan for at least 28 inches of depth from wall to front edge.

Slant-tops offer a more ergonomic playing position — closer to how you sit at a desk than standing at an arcade machine. Many customers prefer this for extended play sessions.

## IGT Game King (Upright)

The Game King shares much of its architecture with the S2000 platform but has a larger video display area.

![IGT Game King multi-game cabinet](https://images.leadconnectorhq.com/image/f_webp/q_80/r_1200/u_https://assets.cdn.filesafe.space/hvLhtyPPvOhUoiGCUbE2/media/687a200ece9e185839e98449.jpeg)

- **Width:** 21 inches
- **Depth:** 22 inches
- **Height:** 48 inches
- **Weight:** 275 to 300 pounds

Slightly taller and heavier than the standard S2000 due to the larger CRT monitor. The extra height accommodates the video display plus the game selection interface above it. Still fits through standard doorways without issue.

## IGT Game King (Slant-Top)

- **Machine only width:** 25 inches
- **Machine only depth:** 25 inches
- **Machine only height:** 20 inches
- **On standard stand — total height:** 44 to 50 inches
- **Weight (machine only):** 160 to 180 pounds
- **Weight (with stand):** 210 to 240 pounds

The slant-top Game King is one of the wider slant-top cabinets due to its dual-screen configuration (main game screen plus a smaller top screen for game selection or bonus displays). Make sure you have at least 28 inches of clear width for moving it through doorways.

## Bally Alpha (Upright)

Bally's Alpha series cabinets are slightly larger than IGT's offerings, reflecting their bigger displays and more modern design language.

- **Width:** 23 inches
- **Depth:** 24 inches
- **Height:** 52 inches
- **Weight:** 275 to 325 pounds

The Alpha is noticeably taller than the S2000 — nearly a foot more. That height accommodates the large LCD display panel and the top-mounted bonus display or signage. At 52 inches, it approaches kitchen-counter height and requires standard 8-foot ceilings with no concern.

The 23-inch width still clears standard 30-inch doorways, but with less margin. If you have a 24-inch door opening anywhere in your path (unusual but possible in older homes), measure first.

## Bally S6000

The older Bally S6000 platform is comparable to the IGT S2000 in its form factor.

- **Width:** 21 inches
- **Depth:** 23 inches
- **Height:** 47 inches
- **Weight:** 260 to 285 pounds

Very similar dimensions to the S2000. The extra inch of depth is due to Bally's slightly deeper power supply compartment. No clearance concerns for standard doorways.

## WMS Bluebird (Upright)

The Williams Bluebird is one of the larger cabinets you will encounter. It was designed to stand out on a casino floor, and it succeeds — at the cost of a bigger footprint.

- **Width:** 24 inches
- **Depth:** 26 inches
- **Height:** 55 to 60 inches (varies by top-box configuration)
- **Weight:** 300 to 350 pounds

The Bluebird is the big one. At up to 5 feet tall and 350 pounds, this is the most demanding cabinet to move and accommodate. The width still clears standard doorways, but the 26-inch depth means you need more room behind the machine for airflow and cable access.

The variable height depends on whether the machine has a top-mounted bonus wheel, a secondary display, or just a basic signage panel. Bluebirds with spinning bonus wheels can reach 65 inches or more.

## Aristocrat MK6 (Mark VI)

The Australian-designed MK6 is a mid-size cabinet with a distinctive curved top.

- **Width:** 22 inches
- **Depth:** 24 inches
- **Height:** 50 inches
- **Weight:** 260 to 290 pounds

The MK6 falls between the compact IGT S2000 and the larger Bally Alpha in most dimensions. The curved top gives it a distinctive profile. Well-suited for home installations with standard ceilings.

## Doorway Clearance Requirements

This is where installations succeed or fail. Measure every doorway, hallway, and turn the machine must navigate from your curb to its final position.

### Standard Interior Door: 30 inches wide, 80 inches tall

Every machine listed above fits through a standard 30-inch interior door. The widest cabinet (Bally Alpha at 23 inches, WMS Bluebird at 24 inches) clears with 6 to 7 inches to spare.

### Standard Exterior Door: 36 inches wide, 80 inches tall

All machines fit easily through standard exterior doors.

### Narrow Doors to Watch For

- Bathroom doors (often 24 inches) — do not route through a bathroom
- Closet doors (often 24 inches) — same
- Some older home interior doors (may be 26 or 28 inches) — all machines still fit, but barely for the Bluebird
- Basement stairway doors — measure these specifically, they vary widely

### Hallway Turns

The challenge is not usually the doorway width. It is navigating a turn in a hallway with a 4-foot-tall, 22-inch-deep machine on a dolly. If you have a 90-degree turn in a hallway, you need the hallway to be at least as wide as the machine is deep (22 to 26 inches depending on model) plus maneuvering room.

For a detailed guide on the actual moving process, see our article on [how to move a slot machine into your home](/blog/how-to-move-a-slot-machine-into-your-home).

## Floor Load Considerations

Slot machines are heavy, but they are not unreasonably so. A 300-pound machine on a 20x22 inch footprint creates a load of approximately 0.68 PSI (pounds per square inch). For comparison, a 200-pound person standing on one foot creates approximately 6.5 PSI.

Residential floors are designed to handle 40 PSF (pounds per square foot) live load minimum per building codes. A 300-pound machine on a 3-square-foot footprint creates 100 PSF — above the minimum design load.

What does this mean practically?

### First Floor (Ground Level)

No concerns whatsoever. First floors sit on a concrete slab or are directly supported by the foundation. You could put ten machines on a first floor without structural worry.

### Second Floor

Place the machine near a wall or directly above a first-floor support wall. Avoid placing it in the center of a large open span. The joists along the walls are supported at their endpoints and handle point loads better. For most standard residential construction, a single slot machine on the second floor is fine in any location. Two or more on the same floor — put them along walls.

### Basement

Basements typically have concrete floors. No weight concerns at all. The challenge with basements is getting the machine down the stairs. See our [moving guide](/blog/how-to-move-a-slot-machine-into-your-home) for stairway strategies.

## Ceiling Height Requirements

Standard residential ceilings are 8 feet (96 inches). Even the tallest standard machine (WMS Bluebird at 60 inches) leaves 36 inches of clearance above. No concerns for any standard installation.

Watch out for these exceptions:

- **Basement ceilings** may be as low as 7 feet (84 inches). Still fine for all machines, but verify.
- **Dropped ceilings** with HVAC ducting can reduce clearance to 6.5 feet (78 inches). All standard machines still fit.
- **Bluebirds with bonus wheels** at 65 inches need 65+ inches of clear space above the floor. Fine in virtually all settings.
- **Under stairs or angled ceilings** — measure the specific location. A Bluebird will not fit under a staircase, but an S2000 at 46 inches probably will.

## Stand Dimensions for Slant-Top Machines

If you are buying a slant-top, you need to account for the stand as well.

### Standard Metal Stand

- **Width:** matches machine (24 to 26 inches)
- **Depth:** 20 to 24 inches
- **Height:** 24 to 28 inches
- **Weight:** 30 to 45 pounds

### Base Cabinet with Storage

- **Width:** matches machine (24 to 26 inches)
- **Depth:** 22 to 26 inches
- **Height:** 26 to 30 inches
- **Weight:** 50 to 75 pounds

Base cabinets add storage for tokens, manuals, and spare parts. They also add visual weight and make the machine look more like a complete piece of furniture rather than equipment on a stand.

## Planning Your Space

Here are our recommendations for a comfortable installation.

### Single Machine Setup

- Allow 30 inches of width (machine plus 4 to 5 inches on each side for air and access)
- Allow 30 inches of depth (machine plus 6 inches behind for cables and airflow)
- Allow at least 36 inches in front of the machine for a stool and player space
- Total footprint: approximately 30 inches wide x 66 inches deep (2.5 feet x 5.5 feet)

### Multiple Machine Row

- Allow 24 inches per machine with 2 to 3 inches between units
- Three machines in a row: approximately 78 inches (6.5 feet) of wall space
- Same depth requirements as single machine

### Electrical Access

Position the machine within 6 feet of an outlet. The power cord on most machines is 6 feet long. If you need to use an extension cord, use a heavy-duty 12-gauge cord rated for 15 amps — not a standard household extension cord.

For everything you need to know about setup after the machine arrives, read our guide on [how to set up your slot machine at home](/blog/how-to-set-up-your-slot-machine-at-home).

## Quick Reference Chart

### Uprights

- **IGT S2000:** 20.5"W x 22"D x 46"H — 250-275 lbs
- **IGT Game King:** 21"W x 22"D x 48"H — 275-300 lbs
- **Bally Alpha:** 23"W x 24"D x 52"H — 275-325 lbs
- **Bally S6000:** 21"W x 23"D x 47"H — 260-285 lbs
- **WMS Bluebird:** 24"W x 26"D x 55-60"H — 300-350 lbs
- **Aristocrat MK6:** 22"W x 24"D x 50"H — 260-290 lbs

### Slant-Tops (on stand)

- **IGT S2000 Slant:** 24"W x 26"D x 42-48"H — 200-225 lbs total
- **IGT Game King Slant:** 25"W x 25"D x 44-50"H — 210-240 lbs total

## Still Not Sure It Will Fit?

[Contact us](/contact) with your room dimensions and which machine you are interested in. We will tell you exactly whether it works and suggest alternatives if it does not. We have helped thousands of customers plan their installations over 33 years — there is no scenario we have not seen.

Ready to find your machine? Browse our [full inventory of 334 machines](/shop), read the [complete buyer's guide](/blog/complete-slot-machine-buyers-guide), and take advantage of our [1-year warranty](/warranty) and [free nationwide shipping](/shipping).
author: Used Slot Shop

title: Video Poker Machines for Home: The Complete Buyer's Guide (2026)
excerpt: Video poker machines deliver strategy-based gameplay that regular slot machines can't match. Learn which models to buy, what IGT Game King versions offer, and what to pay for a used machine.
body:
Whether you call it a video poker machine or a poker slot, this style of gaming cabinet has been drawing a dedicated following for decades. Unlike regular slot machines where every spin is purely random and there is no decision to make, video poker puts a 52-card deck in front of you. The cards you hold, the cards you discard — that choice matters. And that is why so many home game room builders specifically seek out a video poker machine for their space.

After 34 years in the used slot machine business, we have sold hundreds of video poker machines to homes across the country. This guide covers everything you need to know before you buy: how these machines work, which games to look for, what the IGT Game King actually offers, and what you should expect to pay.

![Home game room with slot machines — Used Slot Shop inventory](https://images.leadconnectorhq.com/image/f_webp/q_80/r_1200/u_https://assets.cdn.filesafe.space/hvLhtyPPvOhUoiGCUbE2/media/6879ca419227091c55c5cb34.jpeg)

## What Is a Video Poker Machine?

A video poker machine is a coin-operated (or TITO/coinless) gaming cabinet that presents a five-card poker hand on screen. Unlike a slot machine, which spins reels and pays out based on symbol combinations, video poker requires you to make a decision: after the initial deal, you choose which cards to hold and which to discard. The machine replaces your discards with new cards and pays out based on the strength of your final hand.

The pay table is displayed directly on screen, so you always know exactly what each hand pays before you play. Pairs of jacks or better, two pair, three of a kind, straights, flushes, full houses, four of a kind, straight flushes, and royal flushes all have defined payouts.

This is a fundamentally different experience from playing a reel slot machine. There is skill involved. There is a correct way to play every hand. Strategy cards exist for every video poker variant, and players who study and follow optimal strategy will get the best possible return from the machine.

## Why Home Game Room Owners Love Video Poker

The strategy element is the biggest draw. A lot of slot machine buyers tell us they also want something where their choices matter. Video poker delivers that without the complexity of a full poker table. You play alone, at your own pace, without needing a dealer or other players.

A few other reasons video poker machines are popular for home use:

- **Multiple games in one cabinet.** Modern multi-game video poker machines offer dozens of variants. One machine can replace an entire poker room.
- **Adjustable denominations.** Most multi-game video poker machines are multi-denominational, letting you choose penny, nickel, quarter, dollar, and sometimes five-dollar play.
- **No tokens required on newer machines.** TITO (ticket-in, ticket-out) machines accept bills and print tickets, making them easy to load with whatever amount you want to play with.
- **Compact footprint.** Slant-top models sit on a counter or dedicated gaming table; uprights stand independently. Both fit easily in a game room.
- **Quieter than reel slots.** Compared to the bells and spinning reels of a loud slot cabinet, video poker machines tend to have a more subdued sound profile — a plus in a shared living space.

## The Most Popular Video Poker Games

The best multi-game video poker machines offer a wide menu of variants. Here are the ones we see buyers request most.

### Jacks or Better

Jacks or Better is the baseline video poker game and the one most strategy guides are written around. The minimum winning hand is a pair of jacks. The best machines offer a 9/6 pay table — full houses pay 9-to-1 and flushes pay 6-to-1. With optimal strategy, the theoretical return on a full-pay 9/6 Jacks or Better machine is approximately 99.5%.

Royal flush pays 800-for-1 per credit at maximum bet levels — which means 4,000 credits when you bet 5. Always bet the maximum to qualify for the full royal flush bonus.

### Deuces Wild

All four 2s are wild cards in Deuces Wild, which completely changes the strategy. Because wild cards make strong hands easier to achieve, the pay table starts paying at three of a kind rather than a pair. Full-pay Deuces Wild machines can theoretically return over 100% with perfect play, though such full-pay versions are increasingly rare on commercial casino floors — which makes home ownership all the more appealing for serious players.

### Double Double Bonus

This is the high-variance, high-excitement version of video poker. Double Double Bonus pays premiums for four aces with a low-card kicker — up to 2,000 credits for a five-credit wager when four aces appear with a 2, 3, or 4. The tradeoff: two pair only returns your original bet instead of the 2-for-1 payout you get in Jacks or Better, which drives wild swings. Players who enjoy dramatic sessions tend to gravitate toward Double Double Bonus.

### Multi-Game Machines

The IGT Game King line packages all of these variants — plus dozens more — into a single machine. Instead of buying separate machines for each game, you navigate a menu screen to pick your game and denomination before each session. This is why Game King machines are, in our experience, the most sought-after video poker cabinet for home use.

## The IGT Game King: The Home Video Poker Standard

IGT introduced the Game King platform and it became the dominant multi-game video poker cabinet in casinos across the United States. When these machines age off the casino floor, they enter the secondary market — and home buyers snap them up.

Here is what makes the Game King stand out:

**Game selection.** Depending on the version, a Game King machine offers anywhere from 20 to 89 different games, covering every major video poker variant plus keno, blackjack, and reel slots. The Game King 6.0, for example, includes 69 games. The IGT G22 Slant-Top packs 71 games into one cabinet.

**Display quality.** Newer Game King cabinets feature a 20-inch 16:9 high-definition display. Earlier models use older CRT or LCD panels, which still work well but are a step down in sharpness.

**Multi-denomination play.** Most Game King machines support penny through five-dollar denominations. You pick your denomination at the start of each game from a menu.

**Bill acceptors.** Game King machines typically include a bill acceptor that takes $1 through $100 bills, plus TITO print functionality. This makes managing credits simple for home use.

**Cabinet styles.** The upright G20 and G22 are the most common. Slant-top variants exist and are popular for buyers who want a bar-top setup.

## What to Look for When Buying a Used Video Poker Machine

Used video poker machines vary significantly in condition. Here is what to check before you buy from any seller.

**The bill acceptor.** This is the most commonly serviced component on any video poker machine. Ask whether it has been cleaned and tested. A non-functioning bill acceptor is a nuisance, though it is replaceable.

**The printer.** TITO machines include a thermal printer that issues tickets. Ask when the printer mechanism was last serviced and whether it reliably prints.

**Button responsiveness.** Video poker machines receive far more button presses per hour than a reel slot, because players are pressing hold and deal buttons constantly. Confirm all buttons are responsive and ask whether any have been replaced.

**Screen condition.** Look for screen burn-in — common on CRT models that ran the same game for years — or dead pixels on LCD displays. A faint ghost image is cosmetic; significant burn-in affects readability.

**Game board and software version.** The game board determines which games are installed. Ask specifically which version of Game King software is installed, as this determines your game menu.

At Used Slot Shop, every video poker machine we sell goes through a full inspection and reconditioning process before it ships. We back that with a [one-year parts warranty](/warranty) and free lifetime technical support.

## Video Poker Machine Price Guide

Used video poker machine prices depend on the model, the number of games installed, and condition. As a general guide based on current market pricing:

- **Single-game video poker machines** (older uprights, one or two games): $400–$800
- **IGT Game King with 20–30 games** (G20 and similar): $800–$1,200
- **IGT Game King 6.0 with 69+ games** (newer displays, larger game library): $1,200–$1,800
- **Slant-top models**: Similar pricing, sometimes with slightly higher demand

Prices shift with market inventory. [Contact us](/contact) for current pricing on our in-stock selection — we carry a range of Game King models and can help match you to the right machine for your budget.

## Setting Up Your Video Poker Machine at Home

Video poker machines run on standard 110V power and plug into a standard household outlet. No special wiring is required for most home installations.

Slant-top machines need a stable, level surface — a sturdy countertop, a bar top, or a dedicated gaming table all work. Uprights stand on their own four feet.

For TITO coinless machines, you simply insert bills and play. For older coin-fed video poker machines, you will need a supply of quarters or dollar tokens depending on the machine's denomination.

A few setup tips we share with every buyer:

- Keep the machine out of direct sunlight to protect the display.
- If you need to move the machine after delivery, always unplug it first and never lay it horizontal — the glass and electronics are not designed for that.
- Clean the bill acceptor sensors every few months if you use the machine regularly.

For a complete setup walkthrough, see our [home installation guide](/blog/how-to-set-up-your-slot-machine-at-home).

## Find Your Video Poker Machine

We currently stock IGT Game King machines and other video poker cabinets in Kingman, Arizona. Every machine ships freight to your door anywhere in the continental United States.

[Browse our current inventory](/shop) to see what is available. If you have questions or want help choosing the right machine for your game room, [contact us](/contact). After 34 years in the business, we have helped thousands of buyers find exactly what they were looking for — and we are happy to help you do the same.
author: Used Slot Shop

title: Pachislo Slot Machines: What They Are, How They Work, and What to Know Before You Buy
excerpt: Pachislo machines are Japanese token-operated slot machines with a unique skill-stop reel feature. Here's what collectors and home game room owners need to know before buying one.
body:
If you have spent any time browsing the slot machine enthusiast community online, you have probably come across the word pachislo. Maybe you saw one on eBay, or a friend mentioned picking one up. Pachislo slot machines occupy a unique niche in the home gaming world — they are not American-style casino slots, they are not simple toy machines, and they are not conventional gambling devices. They are Japanese gaming machines with a distinctive look, feel, and play style that has built a loyal following among collectors and home game room enthusiasts in the United States.

This guide covers what pachislo machines actually are, how they differ from American slot machines, whether they are legal to own, what they cost, and what to know before you buy one.

![Coin and token slot machine components — Used Slot Shop](https://images.leadconnectorhq.com/image/f_webp/q_80/r_1200/u_https://assets.cdn.filesafe.space/hvLhtyPPvOhUoiGCUbE2/media/6879c9c0a6483394c2d2689d.jpeg)

## What Is a Pachislo Machine?

Pachislo (also spelled pachislot or pachisuro) is the name for the slot-machine-style gaming cabinets found in pachinko parlors across Japan. The name blends "pachinko" and "slot."

In Japan, conventional gambling for money is illegal, so pachinko parlors operate on a token system. Players win tokens, then exchange those tokens for prizes at a separate redemption counter nearby. This legal framework has allowed the pachinko and pachislo industry to operate as entertainment, not gambling, under Japanese law.

Pachislo machines are built around tokens, not coins. They do not dispense cash. They are, by design and by law, amusement devices — which has implications for their legal status when they arrive in the United States.

## The Defining Feature: Skill-Stop Reels

The characteristic that sets a pachislo machine apart from every American slot machine is the **skill-stop feature**.

On a conventional slot machine, you press a button and the reels spin until the machine stops them automatically. You have zero control over the outcome.

On a pachislo, each reel has its own dedicated stop button. After you press the spin button and the reels begin spinning, you press each individual stop button to halt that reel. The sequence and timing of those button presses is entirely under your control.

This creates a fundamentally different playing experience. Pachislo enthusiasts talk about developing a feel for stopping the reels at the right moment. It makes every spin an active, participatory event rather than a passive one.

In a home setting, the practical effect is that every spin involves three deliberate button presses. The machine keeps your hands busy and creates a rhythm to the gameplay that pure slot machine play does not have.

## How Pachislo Machines Come to the United States

Japanese pachinko parlors typically retire their machines every one to three years and replace them with newer models. Retired machines enter the secondary market in large numbers. A significant portion of them are refurbished and exported — primarily to the United States, where there is steady demand from collectors and home game room builders.

This export market has been active for decades. Companies specializing in used pachislo importation inspect the machines, replace worn parts, and sell them to US buyers. Because the machines are amusement devices designed around tokens rather than cash payouts, they occupy a different legal category than American casino slot machines.

## Are Pachislo Machines Legal in the United States?

In most US states, pachislo machines are legal to own for home entertainment because they are token-based amusement devices that cannot dispense currency. They are generally not classified the same way as real casino slot machines.

However, state laws vary and local ordinances can apply. Our [state-by-state guide to slot machine ownership](/blog/is-it-legal-to-own-a-slot-machine-state-by-state-guide) covers the legal landscape for American-style casino slot machines; pachislo machines typically fall into a more permissive category because of their amusement-only design. Always verify the specific rules in your state and municipality before purchasing any gaming device.

## What Pachislo Machines Look Like and How They Play

Pachislo machines are typically upright three-reel cabinets, roughly the size of a small upright arcade game. They are more compact and lighter than full-size American slot machines — most weigh between 50 and 90 pounds, compared to 200 or more pounds for a typical IGT or Bally cabinet.

The reels display Japanese-language symbols and graphics, heavily themed around popular anime series, movies, video games, and pop culture franchises. Elaborate animated bonus sequences and dramatic sound presentations are hallmarks of the format — pachislo machines are known for being visually and aurally dramatic in a way that is distinctly different from American casino aesthetics.

**How gameplay works:**

1. Insert tokens into the machine.
2. Press the bet button — most pachislo machines play one to three tokens per spin.
3. The reels begin spinning.
4. Press each skill-stop button in sequence to halt the reels.
5. If the combination pays out, the machine dispenses tokens into the tray.

You play with tokens that either came with the machine or that you purchase separately. There is no cash payout at any stage — just tokens you reuse for more play.

## Pachislo vs. American Slot Machines: Key Differences

Understanding how pachislo machines compare to American casino slots helps clarify which is right for your game room.

**Reel control:** Pachislo machines give you a skill-stop button for each reel. American slot machines give you none.

**Play currency:** Pachislo machines use tokens exclusively. American casino slot machines typically use TITO tickets or coins, and many accept bills directly through a bill acceptor.

**Cabinet weight:** Pachislo machines weigh roughly 50–90 lbs. American casino cabinets typically weigh 150–300 lbs. The lighter weight makes pachislo machines easier to move and place in a room.

**Pricing:** Used pachislo machines are generally more affordable — typically $150–$600 for a working machine in good condition. American casino slot machines generally start at $800–$1,000 and run $1,500–$3,000+ for popular models in excellent condition.

**Themes and graphics:** Pachislo graphics are drawn from Japanese pop culture. American machines are themed around casino brands, classic icons, and licensed games familiar to Las Vegas visitors.

**Legal classification:** Pachislo machines are amusement devices in the US market. American casino slot machines are regulated gaming devices subject to state-specific age restrictions in many jurisdictions.

## Pachislo Machine Pricing

Used pachislo machines in working condition typically sell in these ranges:

- **Basic models, older themes, minor wear:** $150–$350
- **Good condition, popular themes, full working order:** $350–$600
- **Premium condition, highly sought-after themes (major anime franchises):** $600–$1,200
- **Rare or limited editions:** $1,200 and up

Popular themes command a premium because collectors compete for them. Machines based on widely recognized anime or game franchises can sell for significantly more than an equivalent machine with a lesser-known theme.

Compare these prices to American casino slot machines, which typically start at $800–$1,000 for basic models and run well over $2,000 for popular titles in excellent condition. Pachislo machines are the more budget-friendly entry point into home slot machine ownership.

## A Note on Power Compatibility

Pachislo machines were built for Japan's electrical system, which operates at 100 volts. The United States runs at 110–120 volts. The difference is small, and many pachislo machines operate on US household current without issue — but this is not universal, and sensitivity to the voltage difference varies by machine.

If you are purchasing a pachislo machine, ask the seller specifically whether the machine has been tested on US power. Reputable importers test their machines before sale. If there is any doubt, a step-down transformer converting 110V to 100V is an inexpensive solution that eliminates the voltage mismatch entirely and protects the machine's components over the long term.

## What We Carry at Used Slot Shop

Used Slot Shop specializes in American casino slot machines — IGT, Bally, Aristocrat, WMS, and similar manufacturers. We have been in the business since 1992 and have shipped machines to thousands of customers across the United States.

We do not currently carry pachislo machines in our inventory. But we are always happy to help buyers understand their options and find the right machine for their game room. If you want the authentic casino experience — the same machines you see on the floors in Las Vegas — [our shop](/shop) has 334+ machines from the manufacturers you recognize, all inspected, reconditioned, and ready to ship.

[Browse our inventory](/shop) or [contact us](/contact) with any questions. We have been doing this for 34 years and we are happy to help you figure out the right fit for your space and your budget.
